questionTextSource: What should you understand by defensive driving?
explanationSource: Defensive driving means that, on the one hand, you do not insist on your right of way in road traffic and, on the other hand, you also take into account potential mistakes made by other road users. Therefore, you do not have to drive over-cautiously - this could in turn pose a danger of its own and impede the flow of traffic. Driving defensively means preparing yourself as well as possible for dangers emanating from yourself or from outside and thus averting them in good time. You can achieve this, for example, by braking early enough to avoid a rear-end collision in front of traffic lights. Generally driving slower protects you against drivers who misjudge a traffic light phase in the heat of the moment or confuse left and right.
answerASource: Not insisting on your rights
answerBSource: Allowing for other people's mistakes
answerCSource: Stopping as a precaution at every crossroads
questionTextTarget: Defansif sürüşten ne anlamalısınız?
explanationTarget: Defansif sürüş, trafikte kendi hakkınızda ısrar etmemek ve diğer yol kullanıcılarının olası hatalarını da hesaba katmak demektir. Bu aşırı temkinli ve trafiği engelleyecek şekilde sürmek anlamına gelmez. Tehlikeleri erken fark edip zamanında önlemek gerekir.

questionTextSource: What can be the causes of rear-end collisions?
explanationSource: There are several possible scenarios that lead to rear-end collisions. If someone brakes too suddenly and unexpectedly, a collision can occur. Tailgating is also a common cause of rear-end collisions, especially if the driver behind is distracted and unfocused. A sufficiently large safety distance can help to avoid such risks.
answerASource: Unexpectedly heavy braking
answerBSource: Inattentiveness
answerCSource: Driving too close behind the vehicle in front
questionTextTarget: Arkadan çarpmaların sebepleri neler olabilir?
explanationTarget: Arkadan çarpma; beklenmedik sert frenleme, dikkatsizlik veya öndeki aracı çok yakından takip etme nedeniyle meydana gelebilir. Yeterli takip mesafesi bu riski azaltır.
answerCTarget: Öndeki araca çok yakın takip mesafesiyle sürmek

questionTextSource: Which of these is a good example of "anticipatory driving"?
explanationSource: Driving with foresight is essential when it comes to road safety. You should always keep an eye on cars directly in front of you so that you can react immediately if necessary. The same applies to all road users in your vicinity.
answerASource: I respond as soon as possible to probable changes in the road traffic situation
answerBSource: I attempt to identify the intentions of other road users, as soon as possible
answerCSource: I limit my observation of road traffic as much as possible on the vehicle travelling directly in front of me
questionTextTarget: “Öngörülü sürüş” için iyi bir örnek hangisidir?
explanationTarget: Öngörülü sürüş, trafik durumundaki muhtemel değişiklikleri erken fark edip buna zamanında tepki vermektir. Sadece öndeki araca değil, çevredeki tüm yol kullanıcılarına dikkat edilmelidir.
answerATarget: Trafik durumundaki muhtemel değişikliklere mümkün olduğunca erken tepki veririm
answerBTarget: Diğer yol kullanıcılarının niyetlerini mümkün olduğunca erken anlamaya çalışırım
answerCTarget: Trafik gözlemimi mümkün olduğunca yalnızca önümde giden araçla sınırlarım

questionTextSource: You are to drive a motor vehicle with which you are unfamiliar. Why do you have to be particularly careful to begin with?
explanationSource: Every driver is accustomed to the driving behaviour and characteristics of his or her motor vehicle. Every vehicle has different handling characteristics due to its particular centre of gravity, dimensions and weight. Even the clutch, cornering behaviour and braking distance of the same models can differ. The driver must familiarise himself carefully with these characteristics, as well as with the controls of the instruments.
answerASource: - of the arrangement and the function of the controls
answerBSource: - of its braking characteristics
answerCSource: - of its manoeuvrability
questionTextTarget: Tanımadığınız bir motorlu aracı kullanacaksınız. Başlangıçta neden özellikle dikkatli olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Her aracın ağırlık merkezi, ölçüleri, ağırlığı, frenleme ve manevra özellikleri farklı olabilir. Bu nedenle sürücü, aracın kumanda düzenine ve sürüş özelliklerine alışmalıdır.
answerATarget: - kumanda elemanlarının düzeni ve işlevi nedeniyle

questionTextSource: You are driving in a built-up area behind a vehicle with number plates that are not local. What could happen?
explanationSource: If you identify a non-local road user from the number plate, you must expect unexpected manoeuvres due to their lack of knowledge of the area. For example, the driver in question could stop in an open lane to ask a passer-by for directions. It is also possible that the non-local driver may be late in recognising the correct route and therefore brake or signal late before bends, junctions and exits.
answerASource: - could brake unexpectedly
answerBSource: - may switch on the direction indicators too late before turning
answerCSource: - could stop unexpectedly to ask the way
questionTextTarget: Yerleşim yeri içinde yerel olmayan plakalı bir aracın arkasından gidiyorsunuz. Ne olabilir?
explanationTarget: Yabancı plakalı sürücüler bölgeyi iyi bilmeyebilir. Bu nedenle beklenmedik şekilde frenleyebilir, dönüşten önce sinyali geç verebilir veya yol sormak için aniden durabilirler.
answerBTarget: - dönüşten önce yön göstergesini çok geç yakabilir
answerCTarget: - yol sormak için beklenmedik şekilde durabilir

questionTextSource: What can cause unnecessary obstructions?
explanationSource: One avoidable obstruction to road traffic is stalling the engine. This is especially a beginner's mistake, which in the worst case can result in a rear-end collision. However, this can be easily remedied by appropriate practice on secluded roads. In addition, according to road traffic regulations, every vehicle must be refuelled in good time to prevent stalling. Finally, it is also part of your duty as a motorist to drive at an appropriate speed, i.e. never drive so slowly for no reason that you could obstruct traffic and provoke an accident.
answerASource: Stalling the engine in dense traffic
answerBSource: Vehicles proceeding too slowly for no good reason
answerCSource: Motor vehicles breaking down because they have run out of fuel
questionTextTarget: Gereksiz engellemelere neler sebep olabilir?
explanationTarget: Yoğun trafikte motoru stop ettirmek, sebepsiz yere çok yavaş gitmek veya yakıtın bitmesi nedeniyle yolda kalmak trafiği gereksiz yere engelleyebilir.
answerBTarget: Araçların geçerli bir sebep olmadan çok yavaş gitmesi
answerCTarget: Yakıtı bittiği için yolda kalan motorlu araçlar

questionTextSource: Why must you not drive extra slowly for no good reason?
explanationSource: Road traffic regulations prohibit unnecessary slow driving, as it impedes the flow of traffic and greatly increases the risk of accidents: it tempts following road users to make risky overtaking manoeuvres at blind spots. In addition, the risk of rear-end collisions increases drastically due to the resulting speed differences.
answerASource: Because it obstructs the flow of traffic
answerBSource: Because it increases the danger of rear-end collisions
answerCSource: Because it encourages vehicle drivers behind to overtake dangerously
questionTextTarget: Geçerli bir sebep olmadan neden çok yavaş sürmemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Gereksiz yavaş sürüş trafik akışını engeller, arkadaki sürücüleri tehlikeli sollamaya teşvik eder ve arkadan çarpma riskini artırır.
answerCTarget: Çünkü arkadaki sürücüleri tehlikeli sollamaya teşvik eder

questionTextSource: What can present a danger?
explanationSource: Safety risks include incorrectly adjusted headlamps. If they are set too low, they create poor visibility for the driver. Lights that are set too high can lead to extreme glare from oncoming traffic and cause serious accidents. The tread depth of the tires is an important safety factor. Worn tires have a much longer braking distance on wet roads. The legally prescribed tread depth of 1.6 millimeters is not sufficient in terms of safety. At this tread depth, the risk of aquaplaning is already very high. With aquaplaning, the vehicle slips uncontrollably.
answerASource: Worn tyres
answerBSource: Headlights set too high
answerCSource: Vehicle in a spectacular colour
questionTextTarget: Ne tehlike oluşturabilir?
explanationTarget: Aşınmış lastikler, özellikle ıslak zeminde fren mesafesini uzatır. Çok yükseğe ayarlanmış farlar karşıdan gelenleri kamaştırabilir. Aracın dikkat çekici rengi ise güvenlik tehlikesi oluşturmaz.

questionTextSource: How can you tell that you are getting tired while you are driving?
explanationSource: Fatigue leads to prolonged reaction times as well as reduced attention while driving. Its signs include excessive yawning. You can also recognise sleep deprivation by the fact that you find it increasingly difficult to keep your eyes open.
answerASource: Heavy eyelids
answerBSource: Frequent yawning
answerCSource: Quicker reaction time
questionTextTarget: Sürüş sırasında yorulduğunuzu nasıl anlarsınız?
explanationTarget: Yorgunluk tepki süresini uzatır ve dikkati azaltır. Ağırlaşan göz kapakları ve sık esneme yorgunluk belirtileridir.

questionTextSource: What can happen if you ignore signs of tiredness?
explanationSource: You should never ignore the first signs of tiredness, such as dwindling concentration, because you may be overcome by microsleep as a result. This in turn often leads to serious, sometimes even life-threatening driving errors.
answerASource: Microsleep
answerBSource: Lack of concentration
answerCSource: Driving mistakes
questionTextTarget: Yorgunluk belirtilerini görmezden gelirseniz ne olabilir?
explanationTarget: Yorgunluk belirtilerini görmezden gelirseniz konsantrasyon azalır, mikro uyku ortaya çıkabilir ve ciddi sürüş hataları meydana gelebilir.

questionTextSource: What should you do if you start feeling tired while driving?
explanationSource: Since tiredness can lead to dangerous situations during car journeys, you should plan sufficient breaks for longer journeys. During this recovery phase, a walk in the fresh air is recommended to get your circulation going again.
answerASource: Take a break straightaway
answerBSource: Get out of the car and move around in the fresh air
answerCSource: Listen to stimulating music
questionTextTarget: Sürüş sırasında yorgunluk hissetmeye başlarsanız ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yorgunluk tehlikeli durumlara yol açabilir. Bu nedenle hemen mola verilmeli ve temiz havada hareket edilmelidir.
answerBTarget: Araçtan inip temiz havada hareket etmek

questionTextSource: How does tiredness affect your driving?
explanationSource: Fatigue at the wheel increases the risk of accidents for the following reasons: concentration and attention noticeably decreases; perception is limited, reaction is significantly delayed.
answerASource: Decreases alertness
answerBSource: Limits awareness
answerCSource: Delays reactions
questionTextTarget: Yorgunluk sürüşünüzü nasıl etkiler?
explanationTarget: Yorgunluk dikkati azaltır, algıyı sınırlar ve tepkileri geciktirir.

questionTextSource: How could a dangerous situation arise?
explanationSource: A major cause of danger in road traffic is driving too fast. This is especially true for beginners, who then easily lose control of their vehicle. Other road users are often not prepared for an unexpectedly high speed. But driving too slowly can also be a hazard, as risky overtaking manoeuvres and rear-end collisions become more frequent in this case. In addition, stationary vehicles can become a dangerous obstacle. To be seen better by other drivers, it helps to switch on your vehicle's lights even during the day.
answerASource: Through vehicles at a standstill
answerBSource: Through failure to maintain suitable speed
answerCSource: By having lights turned on during day
questionTextTarget: Tehlikeli bir durum nasıl ortaya çıkabilir?
explanationTarget: Tehlikeler; duran araçlardan, uygun hızın seçilmemesinden ve çok hızlı veya çok yavaş sürüşten doğabilir. Gündüz far yakmak ise genellikle görünürlüğü artırır.

questionTextSource: What could cause the vehicle to leave the road?
explanationSource: Drivers often drift off the road due to inattention or lack of concentration. Distractions, such as talking on a mobile phone, can also lead to such driving errors. A third cause of leaving the road is fatigue combined with microsleep.
answerASource: Distraction
answerBSource: Inattention
answerCSource: Tiredness
questionTextTarget: Aracın yoldan çıkmasına ne sebep olabilir?
explanationTarget: Dikkatin dağılması, dalgınlık ve yorgunluk aracın yoldan çıkmasına sebep olabilir.

questionTextSource: What is a frequent cause of serious accidents?
explanationSource: Serious accidents often result: from risky overtaking manoeuvres and rear-end collisions due to unnecessary slow driving, from fatigue and delayed reactions, and from children acting unpredictably and rashly.
answerASource: Failure to pay attention to other road traffic
answerBSource: Driving too close to the vehicle in front
answerCSource: Failure to adjust speed accordingly
questionTextTarget: Ciddi kazaların sık görülen nedeni nedir?
explanationTarget: Ciddi kazalara çoğu zaman dikkatsizlik, öndeki araca çok yakın takip mesafesi ve hızın duruma göre ayarlanmaması sebep olur.

questionTextSource: What should you reckon with when approaching children?
explanationSource: Children can always behave ill-considered and are therefore exempt from the principle of trust towards other road users. Thus, when children approach you, you must reduce your speed to be ready to brake.
answerASource: Rash behaviour on the part of the children
answerBSource: Fast and correct reactions of the children
answerCSource: Proper road conduct of the children
questionTextTarget: Çocuklara yaklaşırken neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Çocuklar düşüncesiz ve öngörülemez davranabilir. Bu nedenle onlara yaklaşırken hızınızı azaltmalı ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What conduct must you expect of children at zebra crossings?
explanationSource: Children generally do not have the necessary training to always behave according to the rules in road traffic. Therefore, it is quite conceivable that they do not know the meaning of a zebra crossing and enter the road despite approaching vehicles or suddenly run back after crossing.
answerASource: They will run onto the zebra crossing without paying attention to traffic
answerBSource: They will turn around on the zebra crossing for no apparent reason and run back
answerCSource: They will wait at the curb because they always correctly judge the speed and distance of approaching vehicles
questionTextTarget: Yaya geçitlerinde çocuklardan hangi davranışları beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Çocuklar trafik kurallarını her zaman doğru değerlendiremeyebilir. Yaya geçidine trafiğe dikkat etmeden çıkabilir veya geçit üzerinde aniden geri dönebilirler.
answerATarget: Trafiğe dikkat etmeden yaya geçidine koşarlar
answerBTarget: Belirgin bir sebep olmadan yaya geçidinde geri dönerler
answerCTarget: Kaldırım kenarında beklerler, çünkü yaklaşan araçların hızını ve mesafesini her zaman doğru değerlendirirler

questionTextSource: What must you do?
explanationSource: If a child suddenly runs across the road without paying attention to the traffic, you should immediately slow down and continue driving ready to brake. Also, make sure that other children follow. It is essential to stay at walking pace to be able to stop completely if necessary.
answerASource: Brake heavily immediately and remain ready to brake
answerBSource: Continue at the same speed because the children will certainly stop
answerCSource: Proceed at the same speed so as not to obstruct the traffic following behind
questionTextTarget: Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Bir çocuk aniden yola koşarsa derhal güçlü şekilde frenlemeli, frenlemeye hazır kalmalı ve diğer çocukların da gelebileceğini hesaba katmalısınız.
answerATarget: Derhal güçlü şekilde frenlemek ve frenlemeye hazır kalmak
answerBTarget: Aynı hızla devam etmek, çünkü çocuklar kesinlikle duracaktır
answerCTarget: Arkadan gelen trafiği engellememek için aynı hızla devam etmek

questionTextSource: What do you have to reckon with when children are waiting at traffic lights?
explanationSource: Due to a lack of life experience as well as insufficient education, children do not always behave according to the rules in road traffic and, for example, get into the lane during a scuffle. Furthermore, you must expect children to suddenly run across the road, even if the traffic lights are still red or the lights of the cross-traffic are just changing.
answerASource: - will already start running when the traffic lights in my driving direction change from green to amber
answerBSource: - will also start running when the light is red because they cannot wait since it's taking them too long
answerCSource: - will push each other onto the roadway
questionTextTarget: Trafik ışığında bekleyen çocuklarda neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Çocuklar sabırsız davranabilir, kırmızı ışıkta koşmaya başlayabilir veya birbirlerini yola itebilirler.
answerATarget: - benim yönümdeki ışık yeşilden sarıya döndüğünde koşmaya başlayabilirler
answerBTarget: - beklemek onlara uzun geldiği için kırmızı ışıkta da koşmaya başlayabilirler

questionTextSource: A group of children are cycling on a cycle path that ends. What do you have to reckon with?
explanationSource: Children in particular, who are generally not overly familiar with traffic rules, tend to make unpredictable manoeuvres. Therefore, especially at the end of a cycle path, you should be aware that the young road users could drive onto the road without warning. Furthermore, it often happens that children ride in groups next to each other and thus partially swerve into your lane.
answerASource: - will move onto the roadway without paying attention
answerBSource: - will move too far onto the roadway
answerCSource: - will get off their bicycles and wait until the roadway is clear
questionTextTarget: Bir grup çocuk, biten bir bisiklet yolunda bisiklet sürüyor. Neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Çocuklar bisiklet yolunun sonunda uyarı vermeden yola çıkabilir veya grup halinde giderken şeridinize fazla taşabilirler.
answerCTarget: - bisikletlerinden inip yol boşalana kadar beklerler

questionTextSource: Which statements are correct?
explanationSource: If you do not obstruct or endanger the passengers getting on or off the tram, you may pass the stopping tram slowly at walking speed. It is important to note that passengers have absolute priority in this situation and do not have to let moving traffic pass. If necessary, you as the vehicle driver must stop and wait.
answerASource: You may pass on the right-hand side at walking speed provided that passengers are not obstructed and you are sure no-one is endangered
answerBSource: If no-one is obstructed or endangered, you may pass on the right-hand side at a speed faster than walking speed
answerCSource: Passengers must allow moving traffic to pass before boarding
questionTextTarget: Hangi ifadeler doğrudur?
explanationTarget: Tramvay durakta duruyorsa, yolcuları engellemeden ve tehlikeye atmadan sağdan yürüme hızında geçebilirsiniz. Yolcuların önceliği vardır; gerekirse durup beklemelisiniz.
answerATarget: Yolcular engellenmez ve kimsenin tehlikeye girmediğinden eminseniz sağ taraftan yürüme hızında geçebilirsiniz
answerBTarget: Kimse engellenmez veya tehlikeye girmezse sağ taraftan yürüme hızından daha hızlı geçebilirsiniz
answerCTarget: Yolcular binmeden önce hareket halindeki trafiğe yol vermelidir

questionTextSource: What must you heed in this situation?
explanationSource: If a public bus or school bus has its hazard lights on and is still moving, you must not overtake it. If the bus has come to a halt at the stop, you may pass at walking pace and at an appropriate distance. This is necessary in order not to endanger the passengers getting on and off the bus. If you are travelling in the opposite lane, you must also slow down or stop to ensure the safety of the passengers.
answerASource: - not overtake the bus while it is still moving
answerBSource: - pass the stationary bus at walking speed provided you are sure that passengers are not endangered
answerCSource: - overtake the bus while it is still moving
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda nelere dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Kamu otobüsü veya okul otobüsü dörtlüleri açık şekilde hâlâ hareket ediyorsa onu sollayamazsınız. Durakta durmuşsa, yolcuları tehlikeye atmadan yürüme hızında ve yeterli mesafeyle geçebilirsiniz.
answerBTarget: - duran otobüsü, yolcuların tehlikeye girmediğinden eminseniz yürüme hızında geçmek

questionTextSource: What do you have to reckon with when elderly persons using walking frames cross the road?
explanationSource: When elderly people with walking aids cross the road, you have a higher duty of care. The principle of trust is suspended. You must expect these people to stop suddenly or turn around unexpectedly. Older people also take a comparatively long time to cross the road.
answerASource: They may stop on the road
answerBSource: They may turn round and go back
answerCSource: They will cross the road slowly
questionTextTarget: Yürüteç kullanan yaşlı kişiler yoldan geçerken neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yürüteç kullanan yaşlı kişiler yavaş hareket eder, aniden durabilir veya geri dönebilir. Bu nedenle daha fazla özen göstermek gerekir.

questionTextSource: A person in a wheelchair is travelling along the road although there is a pavement on the right. What do you do?
explanationSource: Disabled and elderly people sometimes do not behave in a roadworthy manner due to physical limitations. Therefore, please be especially careful and be sure to show consideration. Expect the wheelchair user to suddenly and unexpectedly swerve and drive even further into the lane. In such situations, it is not permitted to use the horn or drive around the person at high speed.
answerASource: I follow behind the wheelchair until I can overtake it
answerBSource: I overtake it, leaving plenty of room on the side because the wheelchair may swing out
answerCSource: I honk the horn to indicate that the person is not behaving correctly
questionTextTarget: Tekerlekli sandalyedeki bir kişi, sağda kaldırım olmasına rağmen yolda ilerliyor. Ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Fiziksel kısıtlamalar nedeniyle engelli ve yaşlı kişiler trafikte öngörülemeyen şekilde davranabilir. Yeterli yan mesafe bırakmalı, arkasından takip edip güvenli olduğunda geçmelisiniz. Korna çalmak doğru değildir.
answerATarget: Geçebileceğim zamana kadar tekerlekli sandalyenin arkasından giderim
answerBTarget: Tekerlekli sandalye yana savrulabileceği için geniş yan mesafe bırakarak geçerim
answerCTarget: Kişinin yanlış davrandığını belirtmek için korna çalarım

questionTextSource: Having crossed the road, a person in a wheelchair cannot get up the curb and onto the pavement. What do you do?
explanationSource: You should help the person. To do this, you may stop and switch on the hazard warning lights to warn other road users of possible dangers. It is not permitted to honk your horn and drive around the person in this situation. This would frighten and unsettle the wheelchair user.
answerASource: I stop and switch on the hazard warning lights
answerBSource: I get out of the car and help the person in the wheelchair
answerCSource: I honk the horn and drive around the person
questionTextTarget: Yolu geçen tekerlekli sandalyedeki bir kişi kaldırıma çıkamıyor. Ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Kişiye yardım etmelisiniz. Bunun için durup dörtlüleri yakarak diğer yol kullanıcılarını uyarabilirsiniz. Korna çalıp yanından geçmek kişiyi korkutabilir.
answerATarget: Durur ve dörtlü ikaz ışıklarını yakarım
answerBTarget: Araçtan iner ve tekerlekli sandalyedeki kişiye yardım ederim
answerCTarget: Korna çalar ve kişinin yanından geçerim

questionTextSource: Who has a severe visual handicap?
explanationSource: You must expect a severe visual impairment in the case of persons with the following markings: If a person is wearing a yellow armband with three black dots and/or a white blind person's cane extended diagonally.
answerASource: The man
answerBSource: The child
answerCSource: The woman
questionTextTarget: Kim ciddi görme engeline sahiptir?
explanationTarget: Sarı kollukta üç siyah nokta veya çapraz tutulan beyaz baston, kişinin ciddi görme engelli olabileceğini gösterir.

questionTextSource: What do you do?
explanationSource: Visually impaired pedestrians often wear an armband and have a guide dog with a white driving harness with them. It is not possible to make eye contact with a visually impaired road user. Therefore, you are obliged to show special consideration here. You must stop to allow the pedestrian to cross the road safely.
answerASource: Stop
answerBSource: Let the man cross the road
answerCSource: Make eye contact with the man
questionTextTarget: Ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Görme engelli yayalar özel dikkat gerektirir. Göz teması kurmak mümkün değildir. Yayanın güvenle geçmesi için durmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: Where buses or trams wait at stops, you must always assume that people will suddenly step onto the road. Some passengers run quickly across the street to reach the bus. When passengers get off the bus, they often enter the lane surprisingly without paying attention to the traffic. Because the reversing light is not lit, you as the driver behind you can be sure that the bus will leave the bus stop driving forwards.
answerASource: Pedestrians crossing the road in front of the bus
answerBSource: People crossing the road from left to right to get to the bus
answerCSource: The bus will reverse suddenly
questionTextTarget: Neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Otobüs veya tramvay duraklarında insanların aniden yola çıkabileceğini her zaman hesaba katmalısınız. Otobüsün geri vites lambası yanmıyorsa otobüsün geri gideceği beklenmez.
answerBTarget: Otobüse yetişmek için soldan sağa yolu geçen kişiler

questionTextSource: What risks must you be aware of?
explanationSource: Cyclists do not always behave in a way that is appropriate for traffic. Here, even if the child has previously looked to your left, he or she could ride onto the roadway. To avoid a dangerous situation, you should therefore reduce your speed and be ready to brake.
answerASource: - proceed onto the road
answerBSource: - continue on the cycle path
answerCSource: - stop moving
questionTextTarget: Hangi risklere dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Çocuk bisikletçiler her zaman trafik kurallarına uygun davranmayabilir. Çocuk yola çıkabilir; bu nedenle hızınızı azaltmalı ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: How do you behave?
explanationSource: Reduce speed and watch the two children very closely. Be careful and ready to brake. Stop if the children's behaviour makes this necessary.
answerASource: watch out for children
answerBSource: be ready to brake
answerCSource: continue driving at the same speed
questionTextTarget: Nasıl davranırsınız?
explanationTarget: Hızı azaltın, çocukları dikkatle izleyin ve frenlemeye hazır olun. Çocukların davranışı gerektirirse durun.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: It is always possible that cyclists do not behave in a way that is appropriate for the traffic. A cyclist could turn left or change sides of the road without indicating this with a hand signal. If there are two cyclists, it is possible that the second will also follow the first without hand signals. Therefore, you should be ready to brake even if the cyclists continue straight ahead.
answerASource: - turn to the left
answerBSource: - go to the other side of the road
answerCSource: - not impede me as I proceed
questionTextTarget: Neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Bisikletçiler sinyal vermeden sola dönebilir veya yolun diğer tarafına geçebilir. İkinci bisikletçi de birincisini takip edebilir. Bu yüzden frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What risks must you be aware of?
explanationSource: If a ball is lying on the road, it can be assumed that a child will come and fetch it. For you as a driver, this means: stop immediately so that the child can fetch the ball safely. With children, you have to expect that they will behave in a way that is contrary to traffic regulations and that they will not be able to assess dangers correctly.
answerASource: the children will not get off the road in time
answerBSource: some children will turn around again
answerCSource: all children will wait on the pavement
questionTextTarget: Hangi risklere dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Yolda bir top varsa, bir çocuğun onu almak için gelebileceğini varsaymalısınız. Çocuklar tehlikeyi doğru değerlendiremeyebilir. Gerekirse hemen durmalısınız.

questionTextSource: How should you behave?
explanationSource: The rule here is: reduce speed immediately, be ready to brake and drive slowly past the situation. It can be assumed that children who are playing are not paying attention to the traffic and are not behaving in a way that is appropriate for the traffic.
answerASource: slow to walking pace
answerBSource: remain ready to brake
answerCSource: continue driving at same speed
questionTextTarget: Nasıl davranmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Oynayan çocuklar trafiğe dikkat etmeyebilir. Hemen hızınızı azaltın, frenlemeye hazır olun ve durumu yavaşça geçin.

questionTextSource: How must you behave?
explanationSource: Most likely, a child will come running into the road after the ball without paying attention to the traffic. Immediately slow your vehicle down to walking speed. Be especially alert and ready to brake!
answerASource: slow to walking pace immediately
answerBSource: remain ready to brake
answerCSource: drive past at the same speed
questionTextTarget: Nasıl davranmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Bir çocuk topun peşinden yola koşabilir. Aracınızı hemen yürüme hızına düşürün, özellikle dikkatli olun ve frenlemeye hazır kalın.

questionTextSource: In this situation, which vehicles may drive past, at walking speed only, while ensuring there is no danger to any passengers?
explanationSource: When the hazard warning lights of a bus are switched on at a bus stop, all vehicles in both directions must pass the bus at walking speed. There shall be no danger to or obstruction of passengers boarding or alighting the bus.
answerASource: - are travelling in the same direction
answerBSource: - are oncoming
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda, hangi araçlar yalnızca yürüme hızında ve yolculara tehlike olmadığından emin olarak geçebilir?
explanationTarget: Durakta dörtlüleri açık olan otobüsü, her iki yöndeki tüm araçlar yalnızca yürüme hızında geçebilir. Yolcular tehlikeye atılmamalı veya engellenmemelidir.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: Since there are children both on the left and right on the street, you should brake to walking pace and remain ready to brake.
answerASource: I slow down to walking pace
answerBSource: I remain ready to brake
answerCSource: I overtake [the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yolun hem solunda hem sağında çocuklar olduğu için yürüme hızına kadar yavaşlamalı ve frenlemeye hazır kalmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What should you do in this situation?
explanationSource: Immediately perform emergency braking to prevent a collision with the cyclist coming from the left. Continuing to ride would be dangerous in the situation, even though the traffic lights are green in your direction.
answerASource: I perform an emergency braking manoeuvre
answerBSource: I sound my horn and continue driving
answerCSource: I accelerate
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Soldan gelen bisikletliyle çarpışmayı önlemek için hemen acil frenleme yapmalısınız. Işık sizin yönünüzde yeşil olsa bile devam etmek tehlikelidir.

questionTextSource: Why must you drive slowly here and be ready to brake?
explanationSource: The child behind the van on the left runs towards the roadway. You cannot see whether it will stop. Therefore, be especially attentive and careful in this unclear traffic situation. Drive slowly and be ready to brake.
answerASource: Because the traffic situation is confusing
answerBSource: Because there is a child on the carriageway
answerCSource: Because [the white van] is moving off
questionTextTarget: Burada neden yavaş gitmeli ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Soldaki minibüsün arkasındaki çocuk yola doğru koşuyor. Durup durmayacağını göremezsiniz. Bu belirsiz durumda dikkatli olun, yavaş sürün ve frenlemeye hazır kalın.

questionTextSource: What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: The man is wearing a yellow armband and has a guide dog with him. He is blind or visually impaired and cannot see the traffic situation. Special care must be taken for blind people. Therefore, you must let him cross the road in front of you.
answerASource: I stop and allow the person to cross the road
answerBSource: I drive to the left past the person, leaving sufficient clearance space
answerCSource: I flash my headlights to signal to the person that I am giving way
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Kişi sarı kolluk takıyor ve yanında rehber köpek var; bu, kör veya görme engelli olduğunu gösterir. Önünüzden geçmesine izin vermelisiniz.
answerATarget: Durur ve kişinin yolu geçmesine izin veririm
answerBTarget: Yeterli mesafe bırakarak kişinin solundan geçerim
answerCTarget: Yol verdiğimi göstermek için farlarımla işaret veririm

questionTextSource: Why must you anticipate that children might behave unpredictably in road traffic situations?
explanationSource:
Children often behave unpredictably, which is why other road users always have to count on the unexpected. Due to external stimuli in the environment, small children are constantly distracted from the road and traffic. They continuously let their eyes hike in the area and get stuck with interesting events or objects. The noise perception also only develops in the course of life and is only available to a limited extent in children. That is why children can usually not assign the many noises in road traffic to a certain direction and it is more difficult for them to assess them purely acoustically from which direction a vehicle approaches.
In addition to the perception of the direction, the perception of distances, speeds and braking distance in childhood is not yet fully pronounced. Therefore, children can often only overlook the traffic situation incompletely.
answerASource: - are easily distracted
answerBSource: - are frequently unable to fully grasp the road traffic situation
answerCSource: - are frequently unable to correctly detect the direction from which a sound is coming
questionTextTarget: Çocukların trafikte öngörülemez davranabileceğini neden hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Çocuklar kolayca dikkatleri dağılır, trafik durumunu tam kavrayamayabilir ve sesin hangi yönden geldiğini doğru belirleyemeyebilir. Bu nedenle trafikte öngörülemez davranabilirler.
answerBTarget: - trafik durumunu çoğu zaman tam olarak kavrayamazlar
answerCTarget: - sesin hangi yönden geldiğini çoğu zaman doğru belirleyemezler

questionTextSource: A child is walking on the pavement with a dog on a lead. Can this cause a dangerous situation?
explanationSource: Dangerous situations can arise here. The dog could break loose, and the child could run after him. It would also be conceivable that the dog would run onto the road, and the child would pull along. They should, therefore, drive slowly and be ready to brake.
answerASource: Yes, if the dog suddenly runs onto the roadway and pulls the child along behind
answerBSource: Yes, if the dog tears away and the child runs after the dog
answerCSource: No, because dogs are afraid of traffic
questionTextTarget: Kaldırımda tasmalı köpeğiyle yürüyen bir çocuk var. Bu tehlikeli bir duruma yol açabilir mi?
explanationTarget: Köpek aniden yola koşabilir ve çocuğu arkasından çekebilir. Köpek kaçarsa çocuk da peşinden koşabilir. Bu nedenle yavaş ve frenlemeye hazır gitmelisiniz.
answerATarget: Evet, köpek aniden yola koşup çocuğu arkasından çekerse
answerBTarget: Evet, köpek kaçarsa ve çocuk köpeğin peşinden koşarsa

questionTextSource: What must you heed in this situation?
explanationSource: When public transport and school buses are at a stop and their hazard warning lights are switched on, the following applies: Vehicles may only pass them at walking speed and at a sufficient distance. This applies to oncoming traffic as well as to vehicles travelling in the same direction as the bus. Any danger to passengers must be ruled out by you as the driver of the vehicle.
answerASource: - pass at walking speed only
answerBSource: - pass only if you are sure that passengers are not endangered
answerCSource: - not pass under any circumstances
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda nelere dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Toplu taşıma veya okul otobüsü durakta dörtlüleri açık halde duruyorsa, aynı yönden ve karşı yönden gelen araçlar yalnızca yürüme hızında ve yeterli mesafeyle geçebilir. Yolcular tehlikeye atılmamalıdır.
answerBTarget: - sadece yolcuların tehlikeye girmediğinden eminseniz geçmek

questionTextSource: Which statements are correct in this situation?
explanationSource: Vehicles coming from both directions may only pass public buses, school buses and trams stopped at bus stops with caution. As the bus has not switched on its hazard warning lights, you do not have to slow your vehicle down to walking speed. Be especially attentive in the situation!
answerASource: Cars coming from the opposite direction may pass the bus provided that they do so with care
answerBSource: You may pass the bus provided that you do so with care
answerCSource: As you are coming from the opposite direction, you do not need to worry about the bus
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda hangi ifadeler doğrudur?
explanationTarget: Durakta duran otobüs dörtlülerini yakmamışsa, yürüme hızına kadar yavaşlamak zorunda değilsiniz. Ancak her iki yönden gelen araçlar otobüsü dikkatli şekilde geçmelidir.
answerATarget: Karşı yönden gelen araçlar dikkatli olmaları şartıyla otobüsü geçebilir
answerBTarget: Siz de dikkatli olmak şartıyla otobüsü geçebilirsiniz
answerCTarget: Karşı yönden geldiğiniz için otobüsle ilgilenmenize gerek yoktur

questionTextSource: Which statements are correct in this situation?
explanationSource: If a bus is at a stop and its hazard warning lights are switched on, you as the driver may only pass the bus at walking speed on the same carriageway. Keep a sufficient distance. If necessary, you must stop and wait. The reason for this is the safety of bus passengers, which must always be ensured. This also applies to road users travelling in oncoming traffic on the same carriageway.
answerASource: The bus may be passed in either lane but no faster than at walking speed
answerBSource: Vehicles in both lanes must stop if passengers would otherwise be endangered
answerCSource: The vehicles in the left lane may pass the bus without paying any special attention
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda hangi ifadeler doğrudur?
explanationTarget: Durakta dörtlüleri açık olan otobüs, aynı taşıt yolunda yalnızca yürüme hızında geçilebilir. Yolcular tehlikeye girecekse her iki şeritteki araçlar durmalıdır.
answerATarget: Otobüs her iki şeritten de en fazla yürüme hızında geçilebilir
answerBTarget: Yolcular aksi halde tehlikeye girecekse her iki şeritteki araçlar durmalıdır
answerCTarget: Sol şeritteki araçlar özel dikkat göstermeden otobüsü geçebilir

questionTextSource: In a residential area a ball rolls out in front of your vehicle. How must you react?
explanationSource: If a ball rolls in front of your vehicle in a residential area, you must expect a child to suddenly run into the road. You must therefore brake and be ready to stop, as the principle of trust does not apply in this case.
answerASource: Brake
answerBSource: Continue driving
answerCSource: Avoid hitting it
questionTextTarget: Bir yerleşim bölgesinde aracınızın önüne bir top yuvarlanıyor. Nasıl tepki vermelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Topun peşinden bir çocuğun yola koşabileceğini hesaba katmalısınız. Bu yüzden frenlemeli ve durmaya hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: You are approaching children playing on the pavement. How must you behave?
explanationSource: Children playing on the pavement could unexpectedly run onto the roadway. The principle of trust is therefore suspended. You must reduce speed and be ready to brake.
answerASource: Slow down and be ready to brake
answerBSource: Continue driving as before so as not to impede the traffic behind you
answerCSource: Sound your horn and continue driving
questionTextTarget: Kaldırımda oynayan çocuklara yaklaşıyorsunuz. Nasıl davranmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Oynayan çocuklar beklenmedik şekilde yola koşabilir. Bu nedenle hızınızı azaltmalı ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.
answerBTarget: Arkadan gelen trafiği engellememek için eskisi gibi devam etmek

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should be prepared for the danger of the dog running into the lane and dragging the child behind it, and of the dog breaking loose and the child running after it.
answerASource: - will run onto the carriageway, pulling the child behind it
answerBSource: - will break free and the child will run after it
answerCSource: - will be kept securely under control by the child
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Köpek yola koşup çocuğu arkasından çekebilir veya köpek kaçarsa çocuk peşinden koşabilir.
answerCTarget: - çocuk tarafından güvenli şekilde kontrol altında tutulacaktır

questionTextSource: In this situation, do you have to proceed slowly and be ready to brake?
explanationSource: As the bus is at the stop and flashing on the right, you should only drive past it very carefully. In addition, there is a person standing directly in front of the bus and thus in your immediate vicinity. You must also expect passengers to get off the bus. Other people may approach the bus from the other side of the road and want to get on. You should therefore slow down in any case so that you can brake immediately if necessary.
answerASource: Yes, because passengers could cross the road from in front of the bus
answerBSource: Yes, because pedestrians not alighting from the bus could cross the road
answerCSource: Yes, because the bus will begin moving immediately
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda yavaş ilerleyip frenlemeye hazır olmanız gerekir mi?
explanationTarget: Otobüs durakta ve sağ sinyal veriyor. Yolcuların otobüsün önünden geçebileceğini veya başka yayaların yolu geçebileceğini hesaba katmalısınız. Bu nedenle yavaşlamalı ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.
answerATarget: Evet, yolcular otobüsün önünden yolu geçebilir
answerBTarget: Evet, otobüsten inmeyen yayalar da yolu geçebilir
answerCTarget: Evet, çünkü otobüs hemen hareket edecek

questionTextSource: The tram has just become stationary at the stop. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: You may carefully pass a stopping tram at walking speed - but only if this does not endanger or obstruct any passengers.
answerASource: - may not be endangered
answerBSource: - may not be impeded
answerCSource: - may be warned using the horn
questionTextTarget: Tramvay az önce durakta durdu. Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Duran tramvayı yalnızca yolcuları tehlikeye atmadan veya engellemeden yürüme hızında dikkatlice geçebilirsiniz.

questionTextSource: Why must you pause here?
explanationSource: It's not clear what the child will do. It would be possible for the child to run onto the road without paying attention to the traffic. Therefore, you must carefully pass through: You should drive very slowly and be ready to brake.
answerASource: - the child
answerBSource: - the motorbike
answerCSource: - the off-road vehicle
questionTextTarget: Burada neden beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Çocuğun ne yapacağı belli değildir. Trafiğe dikkat etmeden yola koşabilir. Bu yüzden çok yavaş geçmeli ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: Special caution is always required towards children in road traffic. In this situation, you must expect that the small child will continue to drive onto the road behind the van. Slow down to walking speed and remain alert and ready to brake at all times. Stop if necessary if you are not sure how the child will behave. It would be grossly negligent to just honk your horn and drive on.
answerASource: Slow to walking pace
answerBSource: Remain ready to brake
answerCSource: Continue driving as before
questionTextTarget: Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Çocuklara karşı trafikte özel dikkat gerekir. Küçük çocuğun minibüsün arkasından yola çıkabileceğini hesaba katmalı, yürüme hızına düşmeli ve frenlemeye hazır kalmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: Pedestrians always have absolute priority at pedestrian crossings. Therefore, brake your vehicle in good time and in a clearly visible manner. This way, pedestrians can easily see that you are fulfilling your obligation to wait and that they can enter the roadway. Even if the pedestrians are still standing at the roadside, it would be absolutely forbidden to simply drive on.
answerASource: Brake early, to clearly indicate to the pedestrians that I am allowing them to cross
answerBSource: Continue driving, even if the pedestrians stop briefly
answerCSource: Arc around the pedestrians, to avoid impeding the following traffic
questionTextTarget: Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yaya geçidindeki yayaların mutlak önceliği vardır. Zamanında ve açıkça görülebilir şekilde frenleyerek geçmelerine izin vermelisiniz.
answerATarget: Yayalara geçmelerine izin verdiğimi açıkça göstermek için erken frenlemek
answerBTarget: Yayalar kısa süre dursa bile devam etmek
answerCTarget: Arkadan gelen trafiği engellememek için yayaların etrafından dolaşmak

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: When pedestrians wait at a zebra crossing to cross the road, they have priority. This also applies if they are on the left-hand side of the road. You should therefore brake in good time. Pedestrians must be able to see that you are complying with your obligation to stop. Even if the pedestrians stop first and wait, you must give them the right of way in a clearly visible manner.
answerASource: Wait to allow pedestrians to cross the road
answerBSource: Continue driving, because there is sufficient time before the pedestrians have reached my side of the road
answerCSource: Continue driving, because there are pedestrians on the left side of the road only
questionTextTarget: Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yaya geçidinde bekleyen yayaların önceliği vardır. Sol tarafta olsalar bile zamanında frenleyip geçmelerine izin vermelisiniz.
answerATarget: Yayaların yolu geçmesine izin vermek için beklemek
answerBTarget: Yayalar benim tarafıma ulaşmadan yeterli zaman olduğu için devam etmek
answerCTarget: Yayalar yalnızca yolun sol tarafında olduğu için devam etmek

questionTextSource: What actions should you anticipate at such stops?
explanationSource: Pedestrians are required to wait at such traffic islands. You are therefore allowed to pass the tram even if the tram doors are still open. But because people often behave carelessly when getting on and off the tram, dangerous situations arise time and again. For example, without paying attention to the traffic, passengers simply run across the carriageway to reach the tram.
answerASource: Pedestrians will step off the tram-stop island unheedingly
answerBSource: Pedestrians will run across the road to the tram-stop island
answerCSource: Pedestrians will wait until the road is clear
questionTextTarget: Böyle duraklarda hangi davranışları hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yolcular trafik adasında beklemek zorundadır. Ancak iniş-biniş sırasında dikkatsiz davranıp yola çıkabilir veya tramvaya yetişmek için yolun karşısına koşabilirler.
answerATarget: Yayalar tramvay durağı adasından dikkatsizce yola çıkabilir
answerBTarget: Yayalar tramvay durağı adasına ulaşmak için yolun karşısına koşabilir

questionTextSource: You want to turn right. What must you be prepared for?
explanationSource: If you want to turn right, road users travelling in a straight line here have priority. These may be pedestrians or cyclists who want to cross the road. If a truck turns right in front of you, it is also possible that a pedestrian will let it pass. In that case, the lorry would block your view of the other side of the road and thus of the road users travelling from there.
answerASource: The truck will stop
answerBSource: [The pedestrian] could let the truck pass by and then cross the road in front of me
answerCSource: After the truck has taken the turn, pedestrians could approach from the opposite side of the road
questionTextTarget: Sağa dönmek istiyorsunuz. Neye hazırlıklı olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Sağa dönerken düz giden yayalar ve bisikletliler önceliklidir. Kamyon görüşünüzü kapatabilir; karşı taraftan yayalar gelebilir.
answerBTarget: [Yaya] kamyonun geçmesine izin verip sonra önümden yolu geçebilir
answerCTarget: Kamyon döndükten sonra yolun karşı tarafından yayalar gelebilir

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for?
explanationSource: Where there are many parked cars, you should always drive carefully and considerately. On the one hand, your view is obstructed by the parked vehicles. Secondly, you must always be aware that car doors may be carelessly opened. Other road users may also want to cross the lane between the parked cars.
answerASource: For persons crossing
answerBSource: For visual obstructions
answerCSource: For vehicle doors opening
questionTextTarget: Neye hazırlıklı olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Park etmiş araçlar görüşü engeller. Araç kapıları açılabilir ve kişiler araçların arasından yola çıkabilir.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: The "Children" danger sign on the right makes it clear that increased attention and a special degree of caution are necessary at this point. In addition to children, adults can of course also suddenly step onto the roadway between parked vehicles here, requiring heavy braking.
answerASource: That adults could walk onto the road
answerBSource: That children could appear between the vehicles
answerCSource: That it could be necessary to brake heavily
questionTextTarget: Neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: “Çocuklar” tehlike işareti bu noktada daha fazla dikkat gerektiğini gösterir. Çocuklar veya yetişkinler park etmiş araçların arasından aniden yola çıkabilir; sert fren gerekebilir.

questionTextSource: Why are older pedestrians at more risk than younger ones from road traffic?
explanationSource: Older road users see less well, hear less well, react more slowly and misjudge the speed of vehicles. Be prepared for these people to recognise dangers too late and react too late or not at all.
answerASource: They frequently have poorer sight and hearing
answerBSource: They frequently react more slowly and are less mobile
answerCSource: They frequently misjudge the speed of vehicles
questionTextTarget: Yaşlı yayalar trafikte gençlerden neden daha fazla risk altındadır?
explanationTarget: Yaşlı yol kullanıcıları çoğu zaman daha kötü görür ve duyar, daha yavaş tepki verir ve araçların hızını yanlış değerlendirebilir.
answerATarget: Görme ve işitme yetileri çoğu zaman daha zayıftır
answerBTarget: Çoğu zaman daha yavaş tepki verir ve hareket kabiliyetleri daha düşüktür
answerCTarget: Araçların hızını çoğu zaman yanlış değerlendirirler

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of in residential areas with low traffic volumes?
explanationSource: In residential areas, low traffic levels often give children and adults a feeling of high safety. Some road users are thoughtless here, which often leads to inappropriate traffic behaviour. In such areas, always expect children playing, inattentive cyclists and pedestrians suddenly entering the road. Drive particularly carefully and attentively, as children in particular are unable to properly assess the dangers of road traffic.
answerASource: That children playing run onto the road
answerBSource: That cyclists are inattentive
answerCSource: That all adults obey the road traffic regulations
questionTextTarget: Trafiğin az olduğu yerleşim bölgelerinde nelere dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Trafiğin az olduğu yerleşim yerlerinde çocuklar yolda oynayabilir, bisikletçiler dikkatsiz olabilir ve yayalar aniden yola çıkabilir.

questionTextSource: What actions should you anticipate from persons at the pedestrian crossing?
explanationSource: Zebra crossings have a protective function for pedestrians. They give them priority over other road users. Some pedestrians thoughtlessly trust this protection and cross the roadway without paying attention to the traffic. At zebra crossings, be aware that pedestrians and especially elderly or vulnerable people may enter the roadway without looking left and right. It is also possible that pedestrians will turn halfway around when the traffic lights turn red.
answerASource: They will look neither left nor right
answerBSource: They will unexpectedly walk onto the road
answerCSource: Sometimes they turn back half-way across
questionTextTarget: Yaya geçidindeki kişilerden hangi davranışları beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Bazı yayalar yaya geçidinin korumasına güvenerek sağa sola bakmadan yola çıkabilir, aniden yola girebilir veya yolun ortasında geri dönebilir.

questionTextSource: A pedestrian with a white stick wants to cross the road in front of you. What should you do?
explanationSource: The white cane shows you that this road user is blind or at least severely visually impaired. The stick is also the sign that the person is allowed to participate in road traffic without an escort. You must always treat blind and visually impaired people with increased consideration. Reduce your speed and stop if necessary. Do not honk: you would frighten and unsettle the person.
answerASource: - reduce speed and stop if necessary
answerBSource: - give a warning signal
answerCSource: - continue driving at the same speed
questionTextTarget: Beyaz bastonlu bir yaya önünüzde yolu geçmek istiyor. Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Beyaz baston kişinin kör veya ciddi görme engelli olduğunu gösterir. Hızınızı azaltmalı ve gerekirse durmalısınız. Korna çalmak kişiyi korkutur.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: The "Children" danger sign on the right warns you to take extra care. With many parked vehicles, however, you must always assume that people will step onto the carriageway unexpectedly. Of course, in addition to children, adults can also fail to behave in a roadworthy manner and suddenly appear between the vehicles.
answerASource: That pedestrians will walk onto the road in front of me
answerBSource: That children will suddenly appear between the vehicles
answerCSource: That pedestrians will wait until I have driven past
questionTextTarget: Neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Sağdaki “Çocuklar” tehlike işareti dikkatli olmanız gerektiğini gösterir. Park etmiş araçların arasından çocuklar veya yetişkinler aniden yola çıkabilir.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: In a traffic situation with several parked vehicles and different road users, the parked vehicles impair your visibility. Pedestrians can be expected to emerge between cars to cross the road. It is also possible for people to get out of vehicles or for vehicles to drive off without paying attention to the current traffic situation.
answerASource: That pedestrians will suddenly walk onto the road
answerBSource: That doors of vehicles will be opened
answerCSource: That vehicles will begin moving
questionTextTarget: Neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Park etmiş araçlar görüşünüzü kısıtlar. Yayalar araçların arasından çıkabilir, araç kapıları açılabilir veya araçlar hareket edebilir.

questionTextSource: What is your assessment of this situation?
explanationSource: You should only pass the waiting pedestrian at the side of the road with an appropriate safe distance and very carefully. The same applies to a moped rider who is properly pushing his moped on the right-hand side of the road. If necessary, you can also change to the left lane.
answerASource: The female pedestrian may walk unheedingly onto the road
answerBSource: The pedestrian with the moped may change direction suddenly
answerCSource: I can continue driving at the same speed
questionTextTarget: Bu durumu nasıl değerlendirirsiniz?
explanationTarget: Yol kenarındaki yayayı ve motosikletini iten kişiyi yalnızca yeterli güvenli yan mesafe bırakarak ve çok dikkatli geçmelisiniz. Gerekirse sol şeride geçebilirsiniz.

questionTextSource: A person at the side of the road is wearing a yellow armband with three black spots. What does this armband indicate?
explanationSource: If you see a person at the side of the road wearing a yellow armband with three black dots, this means for you: the principle of trust has been suspended. You now have an increased duty of care. This is because the armband indicates a physical limitation in the form of a severe visual impairment. If visually impaired persons wear this armband and/or a white stick, they are allowed to move in traffic without an escort.
answerASource: The wearer is disabled
answerBSource: I am obliged to behave in a particular way towards this person
answerCSource: This person belongs to a particular profession
questionTextTarget: Yol kenarındaki bir kişi, üzerinde üç siyah nokta olan sarı bir kolluk takıyor. Bu kolluk neyi gösterir?
explanationTarget: Üç siyah noktalı sarı kolluk ciddi görme engelini gösterir. Bu kişilere karşı özel dikkat ve özen göstermek zorundasınız.
answerBTarget: Bu kişiye karşı özel şekilde davranmakla yükümlüyüm
answerCTarget: Bu kişi belirli bir mesleğe mensuptur

questionTextSource: Why do you have to reduce your speed here?
explanationSource: If a van is parked in front of a house and a child runs out of the house, the child may run into the road directly in front of the van. Children rarely pay attention to traffic. Therefore, you should be able to brake immediately in an emergency. To do this, you should reduce your driving speed.
answerASource: - child
answerBSource: - motorbike
answerCSource: - [SUV]
questionTextTarget: Burada neden hızınızı azaltmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Evin önünde park etmiş bir minibüs ve evden koşan bir çocuk varsa, çocuk minibüsün önünden yola çıkabilir. Acil durumda hemen frenleyebilmek için hızınızı azaltmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when pedestrians are crossing the road?
explanationSource: When a pedestrian crosses the roadway, it is not certain that he will continue this action to the opposite side of the road. It is also quite possible that he will stop, turn around or continue with a delay.
answerASource: - be inattentive
answerBSource: - come to a sudden stop
answerCSource: - turn back, after half-way
questionTextTarget: Yayalar yolu geçerken neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yaya yolu geçerken durabilir, geri dönebilir veya gecikmeli devam edebilir.

questionTextSource: Why must you drive slowly now, and be ready to brake?
explanationSource: You must drive slowly and be ready to brake because passengers can cross the road in front of the bus.
answerASource: Because of the pedestrian
answerBSource: Because the bus is moving off
answerCSource: Because [of the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Neden şimdi yavaş gitmeli ve frenlemeye hazır olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Otobüsün önünden yolcular yolu geçebilir. Bu nedenle yavaş ve frenlemeye hazır sürmelisiniz.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to brake here?
explanationSource: A child is behind the parked vehicle and will probably cross the road in the next moment.
answerASource: - child
answerBSource: - cyclist
answerCSource: - [green] vehicle
questionTextTarget: Burada neden fren yapmanız gerekiyor?
explanationTarget: Park etmiş aracın arkasındaki çocuk büyük olasılıkla birazdan yola çıkacaktır.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Since the dog, most likely, will run onto the street from the left, you have to reduce your speed and prepare for a braking for danger.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: Ich ready myself for an emergency braking manoeuvre
answerCSource: I continue driving as before
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Köpeğin soldan yola koşması muhtemeldir. Hızınızı azaltmalı ve tehlike frenlemesine hazır olmalısınız.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop here?
explanationSource: You should stop here because there are 2 pedestrians in front of the bus and they want to cross the road. One from the right and one from the left.
answerASource: Because [a pedestrian] wants to cross the road from the left
answerBSource: Because a pedestrian wants to cross the road from the right
answerCSource: Because [a cyclist] wants to cross the road from the left
questionTextTarget: Burada neden durmanız gerekiyor?
explanationTarget: Otobüsün önünde yolu geçmek isteyen iki yaya vardır; biri sağdan, biri soldan gelmektedir.
answerATarget: Çünkü [bir yaya] soldan yolu geçmek istiyor
answerBTarget: Çünkü bir yaya sağdan yolu geçmek istiyor
answerCTarget: Çünkü [bir bisikletli] soldan yolu geçmek istiyor

questionTextSource: Why are you required to brake now?
explanationSource: The child could run onto the roadway at any moment. Brake to avoid an accident.
answerASource: Because of the child
answerBSource: Because of the [green] car
answerCSource: Because of [the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Şimdi neden fren yapmanız gerekiyor?
explanationTarget: Çocuk her an yola koşabilir. Kazayı önlemek için fren yapmalısınız.

questionTextSource: Where must you particularly reckon with dirt on the roadway and therefore danger of skidding?
explanationSource: At junctions of dirt roads and at construction sites, the roadway is likely to be heavily soiled with soil, clay and dirt. Vehicles coming from there bring the dirt onto the road with the tread grooves of their tyres. In wet conditions, this means an increased risk of slipping.
answerASource: Near construction sites
answerBSource: At junctions with farm tracks
answerCSource: At innercity crossroads
questionTextTarget: Yolda kirlenme ve buna bağlı kayma tehlikesini özellikle nerelerde hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Toprak yolların birleşimlerinde ve şantiyelerde yol kil, toprak ve çamurla kirlenebilir. Islak havada kayma tehlikesi artar.

questionTextSource: Why must the safe distance maintained be much greater on a wet roadway than on a dry roadway?
explanationSource: On wet roads, the adhesion between the tyre and the road is poorer due to the water film. As a result, the braking distance is considerably longer. In addition, the spray from the car in front worsens visibility. The same applies to rainy weather.
answerASource: The contact between the tyres and the roadway deteriorates
answerBSource: Wheel-spray can impair visibility
answerCSource: Braking distances are greater
questionTextTarget: Islak yolda güvenli takip mesafesi neden kuru yola göre çok daha büyük olmalıdır?
explanationTarget: Islak yolda lastik ile yol arasındaki tutunma azalır, fren mesafesi uzar ve öndeki aracın sıçrattığı su görüşü bozabilir.
answerATarget: Lastikler ile yol arasındaki temas kötüleşir

questionTextSource: Where must you especially reckon with ice on the roadway?
explanationSource: You must expect increased icing on the road in the following areas: in damp, shady and windy locations that favour the formation of ice on the road. These include, above all, forest locations and bridges. In forests, the cold lasts longer than in open fields. Because of the shade, moisture takes longer to evaporate here. On bridges, roadways store less heat than on the ground. Bridge roadways are therefore colder and ice up faster. The moisture of the surrounding water and the increased wind due to the unprotected location also promote icing.
answerASource: On bridges
answerBSource: On stretches of road running through forest
answerCSource: On frequently used stretches of road
questionTextTarget: Yolda buzlanmayı özellikle nerelerde beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Köprülerde ve orman içinden geçen yollarda nem daha uzun süre kalır ve yol daha hızlı donar. Bu yerlerde buzlanma daha sık görülür.

questionTextSource: What are the special conditions when driving along tree-lined avenues in the cold season?
explanationSource: The particularities of driving along avenues in the cold season result from the basic characteristics of an avenue: it is characterised by uniform rows of trees running parallel to the roadway on the right and left. The foliage of the trees creates shade on the roadway. This keeps moisture longer, which favours icing in cold weather. If the leaves fall on the roadway, it also significantly increases the risk of the vehicle slipping.
answerASource: Wet leaves as well as white frost, black ice and snowy surfaces are particularly dangerous above all on cambered roadways
answerBSource: White frost and black ice occur more frequently and more unexpectedly on cobbled roadways than on other roadways
answerCSource: Tree-lined avenues are particularly safe in winter because the treetops protect the road from snow
questionTextTarget: Soğuk mevsimde ağaçlıklı yollarda sürerken hangi özel durumlar vardır?
explanationTarget: Ağaçların gölgesi nemin daha uzun süre kalmasına ve buzlanmaya yol açar. Yapraklar da kayma tehlikesini artırır.
answerATarget: Islak yapraklar, kırağı, gizli buz ve karla kaplı yüzeyler özellikle bombeli yollarda çok tehlikelidir
answerBTarget: Kırağı ve gizli buz parke taşlı yollarda diğer yollara göre daha sık ve beklenmedik şekilde oluşur
answerCTarget: Ağaçlıklı yollar kışın özellikle güvenlidir, çünkü ağaç tepeleri yolu kardan korur

questionTextSource: There is oil on the road. What should you do now?
explanationSource: If there is oil on the road, avoid jerky steering and heavy braking; there is a risk of slipping. Provided other road users are not endangered, you can move to the left lane. Notify the fire brigade so that the oil can be bound.
answerASource: I notify the fire brigade
answerBSource: I avoid heavy braking and abrupt steering movements
answerCSource: I swerve onto the left-hand side of the road
questionTextTarget: Yolda yağ var. Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yolda yağ varsa ani direksiyon hareketlerinden ve sert frenlemeden kaçının. İtfaiyeye haber verin. Diğerlerini tehlikeye atmıyorsanız sol şeride geçebilirsiniz.
answerBTarget: Sert frenleme ve ani direksiyon hareketlerinden kaçınırım

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for here?
explanationSource: Due to the weather and the corresponding conditions on the road, you have to be prepared for an extended braking distance, for a vehicle coming from the right (right before left) and for a narrowed lane.
answerASource: A vehicle coming from the right
answerBSource: An extended braking distance
answerCSource: A narrowed carriageway
questionTextTarget: Burada neye hazırlıklı olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Hava ve yol koşulları nedeniyle fren mesafesi uzayabilir, sağdan araç gelebilir ve yol daralmış olabilir.

questionTextSource: You are driving following a heavy rain shower. What should you be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: When it rains, your view worsens. Therefore, you should enlarge your safety distance to the vehicle in front, as your braking distance is extended. Spray water can be whirled up from the vehicle in front by a wet road. This means that bumps are difficult to recognize.
answerASource: I should maintain a large safety distance between me and the vehicle ahead
answerBSource: It is difficult for me to detect uneven areas on the road surface
answerCSource: Splash water might impair my vision
questionTextTarget: Şiddetli yağmurdan sonra sürüyorsunuz. Bu durumda neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Yağmur görüşü bozar, fren mesafesini uzatır ve öndeki aracın sıçrattığı su yol bozukluklarını görmeyi zorlaştırır. Güvenli takip mesafesini artırmalısınız.
answerATarget: Öndeki araçla aramda büyük güvenlik mesafesi bırakmalıyım
answerBTarget: Yol yüzeyindeki bozuklukları fark etmem zorlaşır

questionTextSource: Why does driving on this stretch of road require greater alertness on the part of the motorist?
explanationSource: A tram may come towards you on the tracks, in which case the lane becomes much narrower for you. Tram tracks are also dangerous, especially when wet, because they become slippery. In addition, as there are also cobblestones, the road surface is uneven and has varying degrees of grip. This can increase the braking distance.
answerASource: The road surface is uneven and road grip varies
answerBSource: A tram might come from the opposite direction
answerCSource: Driving on the tracks is dangerous
questionTextTarget: Bu yol kesiminde sürüş neden sürücüden daha fazla dikkat ister?
explanationTarget: Tramvay rayları özellikle ıslakken kaygandır. Karşıdan tramvay gelebilir ve şerit daralabilir. Yol yüzeyi de düzensizdir ve tutunma değişir.
answerATarget: Yol yüzeyi düzensizdir ve yol tutuşu değişir

questionTextSource: The road is icy for a short distance. What should you avoid here, if possible?
explanationSource: On icy roads, your tyres do not grip as well as usual. Therefore, you should avoid dangerous situations, i.e. neither brake nor accelerate abruptly. You should also refrain from sudden steering movements. This could cause your vehicle to skid. Only brake and steer away from the icy surface.
answerASource: Accelerating
answerBSource: Braking
answerCSource: Steering wheel movements
questionTextTarget: Yol kısa bir mesafe buzlu. Burada mümkünse nelerden kaçınmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Buzlu yolda lastikler yeterince tutunmaz. Ani hızlanma, frenleme ve direksiyon hareketleri aracın kaymasına sebep olabilir.

questionTextSource: While driving, how can you safely check whether the road is icy?
explanationSource: Test the condition of the road by braking carefully at low speed. Be sure to pay attention to the traffic behind you. Avoid jerking the steering or accelerating sharply. This could cause your vehicle to swerve and become uncontrollable.
answerASource: By carefully applying the brakes at very low speed
answerBSource: By jerking the steering wheel to the left and right
answerCSource: By rapid acceleration
questionTextTarget: Sürüş sırasında yolun buzlu olup olmadığını güvenli şekilde nasıl kontrol edebilirsiniz?
explanationTarget: Düşük hızda ve arkadaki trafiğe dikkat ederek frenlere dikkatlice basarak yol durumunu test edebilirsiniz.
answerATarget: Çok düşük hızda frenleri dikkatlice kullanarak

questionTextSource: Why must you drive with particular caution here?
explanationSource: Snow makes the road slippery. There is a curve in front of you that is difficult to see and a car is parked there. You will have to pull out into the oncoming lane to drive around it. Therefore, drive extra carefully to be able to react at any time. In addition, your vehicle needs suitable tyres for good grip.
answerASource: Because the roadway is slippery
answerBSource: Because the view is obscured by the bend
answerCSource: Because the roadway is made narrower by the wrongly parked car
questionTextTarget: Burada neden özellikle dikkatli sürmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Kar yol yüzeyini kaygan yapar. Önünüzde görülmesi zor bir viraj ve yanlış park etmiş bir araç var. Hızınızı azaltmalı ve dikkatli olmalısınız.
answerBTarget: Viraj nedeniyle görüş kapalı olduğu için
answerCTarget: Yanlış park etmiş araç yolu daralttığı için

questionTextSource: Where must you be prepared to encounter sudden fog?
explanationSource: Humidity at low temperatures favours the formation of fog. Therefore, fog is more likely to occur around lakes, watercourses and rivers as well as moorland areas.
answerASource: Near rivers
answerBSource: In marshy areas
answerCSource: Near lakes
questionTextTarget: Ani sisle nerelerde karşılaşmaya hazırlıklı olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Nem ve düşük sıcaklık sis oluşumunu artırır. Bu nedenle nehir, göl ve bataklık yakınlarında sis beklenmelidir.

questionTextSource: What do you do if visibility is drastically reduced by fog?
explanationSource: Adjust your speed and driving style to the visibility conditions. Make sure that you can stop within sight. If necessary, increase the safety distance to the vehicle in front. Fog with a visibility of 50 metres, for example, means that you may drive at a maximum of 50 km/h.
answerASource: Adapt your speed in time to visibility conditions
answerBSource: Increase the distance from the vehicle in front
answerCSource: Drive up close to the vehicle in front so you can use its rear lights as an orientation point
questionTextTarget: Sis nedeniyle görüş ciddi şekilde azaldığında ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Hızınızı ve sürüş tarzınızı görüş mesafesine uyarlayın. Görüş mesafesi içinde durabilecek şekilde sürün ve gerekirse takip mesafesini artırın.
answerCTarget: Arka ışıklarını yön bulmak için kullanabilmek üzere öndeki araca yaklaşırım

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action here?
explanationSource: Even if the lane marking is not visible, the traffic signs show the presence of the pedestrian crossing. Brake carefully to prevent your vehicle from skidding.
answerASource: I brake carefully
answerBSource: I allow [the pedestrian] on the right to cross the road
answerCSource: I carefully continue driving, because there are no markings visible on the road
questionTextTarget: Burada doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Yol çizgileri görünmese bile trafik işaretleri yaya geçidini gösterir. Aracın kaymasını önlemek için dikkatli fren yapmalısınız.
answerCTarget: Yolda çizgi görünmediği için dikkatli şekilde sürmeye devam ederim

questionTextSource: Why do you have to pay particular attention here?
explanationSource: There is a long curve in front of you, the end of which you cannot see. A fast-moving vehicle could come towards you from there and cut your lane. The road surface is also uneven and does not have an even surface. So anticipate insufficient grip and drive at an adapted speed.
answerASource: Because it is not clear which direction the road will take
answerBSource: Because the road surface is uneven
answerCSource: Because oncoming traffic could cut the corner
questionTextTarget: Burada neden özellikle dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Önünüzde sonu görünmeyen uzun bir viraj var. Karşıdan hızlı bir araç gelip şeridinizi kesebilir. Yol yüzeyi de düzensizdir ve tutunma yetersiz olabilir.
answerATarget: Yolun nasıl devam edeceği belli olmadığı için

questionTextSource: You want to exit the motorway; to which must you pay particularly close attention?
explanationSource: In the video you see a red motorcycle on the deceleration lane. Later it is no longer visible, because it is located in the blind spot on the right side of you. Now if you also swing out on the deceleration lane, you'll cut the motorcyclist - a dangerous situation for him. Make sure that the rider passes right by you before changing lanes.
answerASource: The red motorbike
answerBSource: The yellow truck
answerCSource: The white van
questionTextTarget: Otoyoldan çıkmak istiyorsunuz; özellikle neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Sağ kör noktada kırmızı motosiklet olabilir. Şerit değiştirirken motosikletliyi kesmemek için önce onun geçmesini sağlayın.

questionTextSource: Why might taking the right turn be dangerous here?
explanationSource: The winter weather makes the road slippery and increases the braking distance at the intersection. Your car or oncoming vehicles could skid or slide away uncontrollably. Therefore, reduce your speed in good time and do not brake abruptly. The road on the right is still full of snow and therefore even slicker. Watch out for pedestrians when turning. They always have priority when you turn.
answerASource: Because I might possibly not be able to stop in time, if pedestrians are crossing the side street
answerBSource: Because vehicles emerging from the side street could start to skid
answerCSource: Because my vehicle might start to skid if I take the turn too fast
questionTextTarget: Burada sağa dönmek neden tehlikeli olabilir?
explanationTarget: Kış koşulları yolu kayganlaştırır ve fren mesafesini uzatır. Siz veya yan yoldan çıkan araçlar kayabilir; dönerken yayalara da dikkat etmelisiniz.
answerATarget: Yaya yan sokağı geçiyorsa zamanında duramayabileceğim için
answerBTarget: Yan sokaktan çıkan araçlar kaymaya başlayabileceği için
answerCTarget: Çok hızlı dönersem aracım kaymaya başlayabileceği için

questionTextSource: You are driving on this road which is not inside a built-up area. What should you prepare yourself for?
explanationSource: On the right you will see the sign for a car park. Expect pedestrians walking away from or towards their parked cars. As the pavements are impassable due to the masses of snow, evasive pedestrians could use the roadway. The weather causes slippery conditions and increases your braking distance. Drive slowly.
answerASource: Pedestrians using the road
answerBSource: The road is narrow
answerCSource: Pedestrians walking only off the road
questionTextTarget: Yerleşim yeri dışında bu yolda sürüyorsunuz. Neye hazırlıklı olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Kar nedeniyle kaldırımlar kullanılamayabilir; yayalar yola çıkabilir. Yol dardır ve kayganlık fren mesafesini artırır.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: The fog is so dense here that you cannot see farther than 50 meters. With such a limited view, obstacles and dangerous situations cannot be detected in time when you're driving at high speed. Therefore, you must reduce your speed to a maximum of 50 km/h and drive very carefully. Turn on the rear fog-lamp of your vehicle.
answerASource: - turn on the rear fog lights
answerBSource: - drive at 50 km/h maximum
answerCSource: - drive at 80 km/h maximum
questionTextTarget: Neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Sis nedeniyle görüş 50 metreden azdır. En fazla 50 km/saat hızla gitmeli ve arka sis lambasını yakmalısınız.

questionTextSource: Why is it necessary to maintain a larger safety distance to the vehicle ahead?
explanationSource: There is a risk of aquaplaning on wet roads. The braking distance is longer than on dry surfaces. The vehicle in front creates a spray of dirty water that reduces your visibility. Also, you cannot see larger puddles of water in advance. To be able to react well to dangerous situations, keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front and adjust your speed.
answerASource: Because spray water could otherwise impede visibility
answerBSource: Because the braking distance is longer than on a dry road
