explanationSource: Basically, you must always expect visibility through your helmet visor to be poorer in the rain and, if it is dry for a long time, a layer of dirt will remain on the road which can form a slippery film on the road surface when it rains, so you must expect the road to be particularly slippery.
answerASource: A particularly slippery road surface
answerBSource: Poor visibility through my helmet visor
answerCSource: A shorter braking distance
questionTextTarget: Uzun kuru dönemden sonra yağmur başladığında neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yol yüzeyi özellikle kaygan olabilir ve vizörden görüş kötüleşir. Fren mesafesi kısalmaz.

questionTextSource: Where does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) occur particularly often?
explanationSource: If the speed is too high and/or there is more water than can be drained from the tire profiles, aquaplaning occurs. A water film prevents contact between tires and the road. The vehicle is no longer controllable. The risk of aquaplaning exists in particular in ruts and road subsidence. Level crossings usually have no increased risk for aquaplaning, since water can usually run well here.
answerASource: In dips in the roadway
answerBSource: Where there are grooves in the roadway
answerCSource: At level crossings
questionTextTarget: Aquaplaning özellikle nerelerde sık oluşur?
explanationTarget: Yol çukurlarında/su biriken alçak yerlerde ve yol oluklarında aquaplaning sık görülür. Hemzemin geçit özel bir sebep değildir.

questionTextSource: What does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) lead to?
explanationSource: Aquaplaning or hydroplaning can lead to neither being able to brake or steer. As a result, conscious driving of the vehicle is no longer possible. It can get into a skid. This effect is caused by a kind of water gore, which slides between the tire and the road.
answerASource: The vehicle can no longer be steered and braked
answerBSource: The vehicle can leave the road
answerCSource: Steering becomes heavy
questionTextTarget: Aquaplaning neye yol açar?
explanationTarget: Aquaplaning sırasında araç artık yönlendirilemez ve frenlenemez; yoldan çıkabilir. Direksiyonun ağırlaşması temel sonuç değildir.

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when it starts raining after a long period of dry weather?
explanationSource: If rain sets in after prolonged drought, a dangerous film of lubricant forms on the road, which is only washed away after a long period of rain. This film is more dangerous than rain alone. Brake very carefully. This lubricating film is also deposited on the windscreen. After turning on the windscreen wipers, it first worsens the view drastically. The braking distance is extended by the poorer traction.
answerASource: A dangerous greasy film on the surface of the road
answerBSource: Bad visibility immediately upon switching the windscreen wipers on
answerCSource: Shorter braking distance
questionTextTarget: Uzun süre kuru havadan sonra yağmur başladığında neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yol yüzeyinde tehlikeli yağlı tabaka oluşabilir ve silecekler çalışınca ilk anda görüş kötü olabilir. Fren mesafesi kısalmaz.

questionTextSource: It starts raining. Why must you immediately increase the safe distance?
explanationSource: When it starts raining, you must immediately increase the safety distance for the following reasons: Under the influence of moisture, the dirt, dust and fine deposits from tire abrasion or pollen on the road turn into a dangerous film of grease. This reduces the traction and thereby extends the braking distance. This lubricating film also forms on the windscreen and on the brake drums. If you use the windscreen wipers, they will first worsen your view and smudge the window. On the brake drums, this lubricating film leads to a slower response of the brakes in wet conditions.
answerASource: Because a greasy film may form which increases the braking distance
answerBSource: Because visibility may become worse and the windscreen wipers do not immediately provide a clear field of vision
answerCSource: Because the brakes react more quickly
questionTextTarget: Yağmur başlıyor. Güvenli mesafeyi neden hemen artırmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Kaygan tabaka fren mesafesini uzatabilir ve görüş kötüleşebilir. Silecekler hemen net görüş sağlamayabilir.
answerATarget: Fren mesafesini uzatan kaygan tabaka oluşabileceği için
answerBTarget: Görüş kötüleşebileceği ve silecekler hemen net görüş sağlamayabileceği için
answerCTarget: Frenler daha hızlı tepki verdiği için

questionTextSource: You are driving in dense fog on a federal road (Bundesstraße) outside a built-up area. Are you allowed to drive in the middle of the road continuously straddling the broken white line?
explanationSource: Driving on the center line is not allowed even in dense fog, especially as drivers can always collide with oncoming traffic due to reduced visibility. If there's fog, slow down and orient yourself on the right side of the road for a safe ride.
answerASource: No, because it could represent a danger to oncoming traffic
answerBSource: Yes, if fog headlamps and rear fog lamps are switched on
answerCSource: Yes, because it reduces the risk of running off the road
questionTextTarget: Yerleşim yeri dışında federal yolda yoğun siste gidiyorsunuz. Kırık beyaz çizginin üzerinde sürekli yolun ortasında gidebilir misiniz?
explanationTarget: Hayır. Yolun ortasında gitmek karşıdan gelen trafiği tehlikeye atabilir. Sis farları bunu serbest yapmaz.
answerATarget: Hayır, çünkü karşı trafiğe tehlike oluşturabilir
answerBTarget: Evet, sis farları ve arka sis lambaları açıksa
answerCTarget: Evet, çünkü yoldan çıkma riskini azaltır

questionTextSource: What must you do on encountering sudden dense fog driving at daytime?
explanationSource: As a car driver you must turn on low beam during the day when there's fog, snowfall and other poor visibility. Because of the insufficient luminosity driving with parking light is not permitted. Adjust your speed to the visibility conditions and use the rear fog light when you can see less than 50 meters.
answerASource: Adapt speed immediately to visibility conditions
answerBSource: Switch on dipped headlights
answerCSource: Only switch on parking lights
questionTextTarget: Gündüz sürerken aniden yoğun sisle karşılaşırsanız ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Hızınızı hemen görüş koşullarına uyarlamalı ve kısa farları yakmalısınız. Sadece park lambası yeterli değildir.

questionTextSource: How can you achieve sufficient visibility through the windscreen?
explanationSource: Turn your windscreen wipers to interval switching, during fog in order to remove fine water droplets from the mist. Turn on your windscreen wipers before overtaking or being overtaken so that obstructions caused by slush or spray water are immediately removed. Avoid fogged windows with the correct ventilation setting: Set ventilation to outside air supply and not to circulating air. Open the windows a gap to let the moisture escape. Briefly switching on the air conditioning dehumidifies the air very quickly.
answerASource: Switch on the windscreen wipers from time to time when it is foggy
answerBSource: When there is slush, switch on the windscreen wipers already before slush is thrown onto your windscreen by other vehicles
answerCSource: When the windscreen is steamed up, switch the ventilator off and keep the windows closed
questionTextTarget: Ön camdan yeterli görüşü nasıl sağlayabilirsiniz?
explanationTarget: Siste silecekleri ara sıra çalıştırmalı, sulu kar gelmeden önce silecekleri açmalı ve cam buğulanırsa havalandırmayı kullanmalısınız. Havalandırmayı kapatmak yanlıştır.
answerBTarget: Sulu karda, diğer araçlar camıma sulu kar sıçratmadan önce silecekleri açmak
answerCTarget: Cam buğulandığında fanı kapatıp camları kapalı tutmak

questionTextSource: You want to overtake on a narrow avenue not within a built-up area. What must you be aware of in particular?
explanationSource: Due to the narrow width of the lanes, even cyclists cannot be overtaken with oncoming traffic. Often, the road is heavily curved to drain rainwater, that means when crossing the center line, the vehicle tilts to the other side. This load change reduces the driving stability. Due to the trees, curves can be confusing. Vehicles with tall bodies often need to travel in the center to avoid colliding with branches and could reach into your lane.
answerASource: Even cyclists frequently cannot be overtaken when there is oncoming traffic due to the narrow width of the roadway
answerBSource: Misjudgements of distances and oncoming traffic frequently result in serious accidents
answerCSource: Vehicles with high superstructures frequently move to avoid branches
questionTextTarget: Islak veya buzlu yolda sürerken ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Ani frenleme, ani direksiyon ve sert hızlanmadan kaçınmalı; hızınızı yol koşullarına uyarlamalısınız.

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: A sudden and strong change of lighting conditions, you must reduce your speed, because the human eye otherwise cannot react fast enough to the change of light. Other road users or obstacles are very difficult to perceive in dark areas.
answerASource: The speed should be adjusted due to the visibility
answerBSource: Judging distances is made more difficult because of the constant changing of light and shadows
answerCSource: The constant changing of light and shadows makes it more difficult to detect other road users in good time
questionTextTarget: Görüş kötüleştiğinde ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Görüş mesafesi içinde durabilecek şekilde hızınızı azaltmalı ve farları uygun kullanmalısınız.

questionTextSource: When overtaking several trucks, you are faced with this situation. What should you do now?
explanationSource: The visibility is very poor due to the weather and the road is not sufficiently visible. In order not to endanger anyone, you should stop the overtaking process as quickly as possible and ensure that you are at a safe distance from the vehicle in front of you when you go back into lane.
answerASource: I abort the overtaking manoeuvre
answerBSource: After pulling back into lane, I again establish an adequate safety distance
answerCSource: I continue overtaking quickly and pull back into lane in front of the red truck
questionTextTarget: Yol yüzeyi kaygansa nasıl sürmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Düşük hızla, yumuşak frenleme ve direksiyon hareketleriyle sürmelisiniz. Ani hareketlerden kaçının.

questionTextSource: You are driving as part of a group of several motorcycles. What reduces the risk of accidents?
explanationSource: When driving together with a group of motorcycles, all should keep their distance behind each other and have enough distance to brake and stop
answerASource: - maintain their position
answerBSource: - assume smaller safety distances
answerCSource: - drive alongside one another in the same lane
questionTextTarget: Birkaç motosikletten oluşan bir grup içinde sürüyorsunuz. Kaza riskini ne azaltır?
explanationTarget: Grup sürüşünde herkes kendi pozisyonunu korumalı ve yeterli güvenlik mesafesi bırakmalıdır. Aynı şeritte yan yana gitmek ve mesafeyi azaltmak tehlikelidir.

questionTextSource: When is there an increased risk of falling during steering and braking manoeuvres?
explanationSource: When turning on bends or braking, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand, dust, gravel, wet surfaces, cobblestones or leaves will increase the risk of falling
answerASource: On wet cobblestone pavement
answerBSource: On wet asphalt
answerCSource: On carriageways with loose chippings
questionTextTarget: Direksiyon ve fren manevralarında ne zaman düşme riski artar?
explanationTarget: Islak parke taşları, ıslak asfalt ve gevşek mıcır bulunan yollarda düşme riski artar.

questionTextSource: Wet road markings increase the risk of a fall. How can you reduce this danger of falling?
explanationSource: Wet markings similarly to tram tracks are slippery. You should cross them as perpendicularly as possible and try to no accelerate or brake on them.
answerASource: Cross road markings at the interstice points
answerBSource: Do not accelerate or brake on markings
answerCSource: Cross long markings at a sharp angle
questionTextTarget: Islak yol işaretleri düşme riskini artırır. Bu düşme tehlikesini nasıl azaltabilirsiniz?
explanationTarget: Islak işaretleri mümkün olduğunca dik açıyla geçmeli, işaretler üzerinde hızlanma veya frenleme yapmamalısınız.
answerBTarget: İşaretler üzerinde hızlanmamak veya fren yapmamak

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: There are two lanes in your direction of travel. You drive on the right side of the two tracks. In oncoming traffic, a snow plow truck approaches. You see in the mirror that a vehicle in the left lane next to you wants to overtake. Snowdrifts hinder you while driving on the lane. You cannot turn left unless the overtaking is completed. Therefore, you must delay further and wait in front of the obstacle until you can dodge to the left.
answerASource: I continue slowing down
answerBSource: I wait before the obstruction
answerCSource: I switch to the left lane now
questionTextTarget: Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Sol şeritte sollayan araç olduğu için hemen sola geçemezsiniz. Hızınızı azaltmaya devam etmeli ve engelden önce beklemelisiniz.

questionTextSource: What should you do here?
explanationSource: In the event of lane damage before a turn, you should reduce your speed considerably to avoid damaging the vehicle. Due to the confusion of the curve, you should not drive past the lane damage on the left, but continue on the right on your lane.
answerASource: - greatly reduce my speed
answerBSource: - continue driving in my lane
answerCSource: - drive to the left past the damaged road surface
questionTextTarget: Burada ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Yol hasarı ve viraj nedeniyle hızınızı büyük ölçüde azaltmalı, şeridinizde kalmalı ve sol taraftan geçmemelisiniz.

questionTextSource: You are driving on the autobahn. What is the correct thing to do in this situation?
explanationSource: Due to the severely restricted view on the motorway, you can switch on your rear fog light and drive a maximum of 50 km / h.
answerASource: - turn on the rear fog lights
answerBSource: - drive at 50 km/h maximum
answerCSource: - drive at 80 km/h maximum
questionTextTarget: Otoyolda gidiyorsunuz. Bu durumda doğru olan nedir?
explanationTarget: Görüş çok kısıtlıysa arka sis lambasını yakabilir ve en fazla 50 km/saat hızla sürebilirsiniz. 80 km/saat fazla olur.

questionTextSource: The electronic stability programme (ESP) display has flashed several times. What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource:
The Electronic Stability Program (ESP) is always active. If the ESP light flashes, it shows you that it is actively intervening in the driving behaviour of the vehicle and stabilising it. Therefore, the speed should be reduced.
If the ESP symbol is permanently lit, it is inactive or defective and needs to be checked.
answerASource: I reduce my speed, because it is too high
answerBSource: I maintain my speed, because the ESP ensures I can continue driving safely
answerCSource: I go to a garage, because the ESP display is indicating a fault
questionTextTarget: Elektronik denge programı (ESP) göstergesi birkaç kez yanıp söndü. Bu durumda doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: ESP lambası yanıp sönüyorsa sistem aktif müdahale ediyor demektir. Hızınız koşullara göre yüksektir; azaltmalısınız.
answerBTarget: ESP güvenli sürüşü sağladığı için hızımı korurum
answerCTarget: ESP göstergesi arıza gösterdiği için servise giderim

questionTextSource: While driving, you use your vehicle’s adaptive cruise control (ACC) system. What must you anticipate in these weather conditions?
explanationSource: The adaptive cruise control system (ASC) independently estimates the distance and speed of the vehicle in front.  This can be limited in bad weather, e.g. due to snow and dirt. Therefore, you must regulate the distance and speed yourself.
answerASource: The functionality of the ACC may be negatively affected
answerBSource: I have to personally control my speed and distance
answerCSource: The ACC stops the vehicle
questionTextTarget: Sürüş sırasında adaptif hız sabitleme (ACC) sistemini kullanıyorsunuz. Bu hava koşullarında neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Kötü hava, kar veya kir sensörleri olumsuz etkileyebilir. Hızı ve mesafeyi kendiniz kontrol etmek zorundasınız.
answerBTarget: Hızımı ve mesafemi kendim kontrol etmeliyim

questionTextSource: You are driving along a narrow avenue. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: The narrow width of the road and its often curved shape due to tree roots force you to take extra care when cornering and when facing oncoming traffic. Choose your speed in such a way that the vehicle can be brought to a halt within half the visible distance. Example: With a visibility of approx. 40 metres, you should choose your speed so that you come to a stop within approx. 20 metres. In purely mathematical terms, the stopping distance at 30 km/h is 18 metres.
answerASource: - be able to stop within half of the distance visible to me
answerBSource: - stop for oncoming traffic, if necessary
answerCSource: - anticipate unevenness at the edge of the road caused by tree roots
questionTextTarget: Dar bir ağaçlıklı yolda gidiyorsunuz. Neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Görüş mesafesinin yarısı içinde durabilecek hızda gitmeli, gerekirse karşı trafik için durmalı ve ağaç köklerinin yol kenarında bozukluk yapabileceğini hesaba katmalısınız.
answerCTarget: - ağaç köklerinin yol kenarında düzensizlik oluşturabileceğini hesaba katmak

questionTextSource: The electronic stability control (ESC) display light has flashed several times. What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: You have to reduce your speed. Due to the slippery road surface and the obviously too high speed in the curves for the road conditions, the wheels lose grip and the car could skid.
answerASource: I reduce my speed, because it is too high
answerBSource: I maintain my speed, because the ESC system ensures I can continue driving safely
answerCSource: I go to a garage, because the ESC display is indicating a fault
questionTextTarget: Elektronik denge kontrolü (ESC) göstergesi birkaç kez yanıp söndü. Bu durumda doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: ESC yanıp sönüyorsa araç kayma sınırında ve sistem müdahale ediyor demektir. Hızınızı azaltmalısınız.
answerBTarget: ESC sistemi güvenli devam etmeyi sağladığı için hızımı korurum
answerCTarget: ESC göstergesi arıza gösterdiği için servise giderim

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate here?
explanationSource:
The snow on the right and left sides of the road may limit the lane. Therefore, you must expect that the distance to oncoming traffic is reduced.
In addition, you can see from the traffic sign that there is a parking area for walkers on the right-hand side. Therefore, you must expect that there are pedestrians on the roadway.
answerASource: Pedestrians on the carriageway
answerBSource: A reduced amount of side clearance from oncoming traffic
answerCSource: That you may be dazzled by the low-lying sun
questionTextTarget: Burada neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Kar yol genişliğini daraltır, karşı trafikle yan mesafe azalır. Sağdaki yürüyüşçü park alanı nedeniyle yayalar yolda olabilir; alçak güneş kamaştırabilir.

questionTextSource: While driving straight ahead, you lose control of your vehicle due to aquaplaning. What is the proper response now?
explanationSource: During aquaplaning, you cannot turn or change the direction of your car. You should also not accelerate or brake. So just carefully take your foot off the gas pedal and try to keep the wheel straight until your wheels touch the road again.
answerASource: I gently take my foot off the accelerator
answerBSource: I keep the steering wheel as straight as possible
answerCSource: I brake firmly
questionTextTarget: Düz giderken aquaplaning nedeniyle aracın kontrolünü kaybediyorsunuz. Şimdi doğru tepki nedir?
explanationTarget: Gazı yavaşça bırakmalı ve direksiyonu mümkün olduğunca düz tutmalısınız. Sert fren yapmamalısınız.

questionTextSource: Why is it dangerous to overtake on this snow-covered carriageway?
explanationSource:
In the event of sneaky, icy road, the tires have less liability and can slip. You should avoid sudden braking through slow, forward -looking driving so as not to skid.
In addition, the in front can also be thrown on the left lane due to the clearing/ stray vehicle in front and your view also deteriorates.
answerASource: Because my vehicle may skid
answerBSource: Because parts of the carriageway may be iced over
answerCSource: Because my vision may become even more negatively affected
questionTextTarget: Bu karla kaplı taşıt yolunda sollamak neden tehlikelidir?
explanationTarget: Araç kayabilir, yolun bazı kısımları buzlu olabilir ve görüşünüz daha da kötüleşebilir.

questionTextSource: While driving at night on a country road you see pedestrians on your side. What do you do if you are dazzled by an oncoming vehicle?
explanationSource: If an oncoming vehicle dazzles you, be sure to reduce the speed so you can stop at any time in a potentially dangerous situation. If pedestrians are on or near the lane, you may even need to stop in such cases. Increasing your speed is not allowed.
answerASource: Reduce speed and, if necessary, stop
answerBSource: Switch to high beam and proceed without reducing speed
answerCSource: Accelerate in order to get out of the area of dazzle as quickly as possible
questionTextTarget: Gece bir köy yolunda giderken kendi tarafınızda yayalar görüyorsunuz. Karşıdan gelen araç sizi kamaştırırsa ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Kamaşırsanız hızınızı azaltmalı ve gerekirse durmalısınız. Uzun far yakmak veya hızlanmak tehlikelidir.
answerBTarget: Uzun farı yakıp hızı azaltmadan devam etmek
answerCTarget: Kamaşma bölgesinden çabuk çıkmak için hızlanmak

questionTextSource: What must you do if wild animals suddenly appear in the full beam of your headlights?
explanationSource: Hold the steering wheel tightly and brake. Dip the high beam, because it dazzles the game and therefore remains on the road. Honk to scare it away. Avoid trying to dodge at high speed. These maneuvers often end at a tree or in oncoming traffic. That's why the following rules apply when there's game on the road: slow down, dip the lights and honk to scare off the game.
answerASource: Immediately dip your headlights
answerBSource: Sound the horn and brake
answerCSource: Proceed on full beam
questionTextTarget: Uzun far ışığında aniden vahşi hayvanlar görünürse ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Hemen kısa fara geçmeli, korna çalmalı ve fren yapmalısınız. Uzun far hayvanı kamaştırıp yolda kalmasına neden olabilir.

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: Due to the large number of trees and the constant change between light and shade, you have to drive particularly attentively, as it is much more difficult to recognise other road users.
answerASource: - I have to drive with particular care
answerBSource: - I may be poorly visible to other road users
answerCSource: - I am more easily able to judge distances
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Işık-gölge değişimi nedeniyle özellikle dikkatli sürmeli ve diğer yol kullanıcıları tarafından zor görülebileceğinizi hesaba katmalısınız. Mesafeleri değerlendirmek kolaylaşmaz.
answerBTarget: - diğer yol kullanıcıları tarafından zor görülebilirim

questionTextSource: In the dark, an oncoming vehicle fails to dip its headlights. What can you do so that you can still see to a certain extent?
explanationSource: If you are dazzled by the light from an oncoming vehicle, look to the right side of the road if possible. This reduces the dazzling effect and you can still stay the lane. It's prohibited to dazzle back by switching on the high beam. Folding down the sun shade is not an appropriate action as it will impair your vision too much.
answerASource: Avoid looking at the headlights of the approaching vehicle and direct your gaze towards the right-hand edge of the roadway
answerBSource: Also switch on full beam
answerCSource: Lower the sun visor
questionTextTarget: Karanlıkta karşıdan gelen bir araç farlarını kısa fara almıyor. Yine de bir ölçüde görebilmek için ne yapabilirsiniz?
explanationTarget: Far ışığına bakmamalı ve bakışınızı yolun sağ kenarına yöneltmelisiniz. Uzun farla karşılık vermek veya güneşliği indirmek doğru değildir.
answerATarget: Yaklaşan aracın farlarına bakmaktan kaçınır ve bakışımı yolun sağ kenarına yöneltirim

questionTextSource: You are driving on a country road in darkness and rain and encounter oncoming vehicles with dipped headlights. What must you remember?
explanationSource: You must not turn on the high beam in this situation, as this can strongly dazzle oncoming traffic. Especially on wet roads, the reflection of the light is greatly increased. Further interferences include drops of water and streaks on the windscreen, which scatter the light and cloud the view.
answerASource: You can be dazzled by reflections on the wet road
answerBSource: Your wet windscreen can result in your being dazzled
answerCSource: You can avoid possible dazzle by switching on full beam
questionTextTarget: Karanlık ve yağmurlu bir köy yolunda kısa farlı karşıdan gelen araçlarla karşılaşıyorsunuz. Neyi unutmamalısınız?
explanationTarget: Islak yol ve ıslak ön cam ışığı yansıtıp dağıtarak kamaşmaya neden olabilir. Uzun far açmak çözüm değildir.
answerBTarget: Islak ön camım kamaşmaya neden olabilir
answerCTarget: Olası kamaşmayı uzun far yakarak önleyebilirim

questionTextSource: What risks could arise if you drive with your main beam lights on?
explanationSource: With high beam on, you can dazzle drivers in front of you and oncoming traffic, which may result in recognizing pedestrians and cyclists not at all or way too late. Game that is crossing the road can also be dazzled, which may lead to the animals remaining in the cone of light. With high beam you can recognize vehicles that broke down better.
answerASource: Oncoming road users will be dazzled
answerBSource: Wild animals on the road will be dazzled and will remain stationary in the light beam
answerCSource: Stationary vehicles on my side of the road will not be detected until it is too late
questionTextTarget: Uzun farlarla sürerseniz hangi riskler doğabilir?
explanationTarget: Uzun far karşıdan gelenleri kamaştırır ve yoldaki vahşi hayvanların ışıkta donup kalmasına neden olabilir. Duran araçlar ise genellikle daha iyi fark edilir.
answerBTarget: Yoldaki vahşi hayvanlar kamaşıp ışık huzmesinde hareketsiz kalır
answerCTarget: Benim tarafımdaki duran araçlar çok geç fark edilir

questionTextSource: Driving at night-time on a rural road, you see persons on your side of the road. What do you do if you are dazzled by an oncoming vehicle?
explanationSource: If you detect people on the side of the road at night as a car driver, you should slow down. Regular eye-contact with the roadside is ideal to avoid an accident. If you are also blinded, you can move to the right and stop.
answerASource: - look towards the right-hand side of the road
answerBSource: - stop if necessary
answerCSource: - turn on my main beam lights
questionTextTarget: Gece kırsal yolda giderken yolun sizin tarafınızda insanlar görüyorsunuz. Karşıdan gelen araç sizi kamaştırırsa ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Bakışınızı yolun sağ tarafına yöneltmeli ve gerekirse durmalısınız. Uzun far yakmamalısınız.

questionTextSource: You are driving on an avenue, with a narrow carriageway. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: On narrow avenues you are only allowed to drive at a speed that allows you to stop within half the visibility if oncoming traffic approaches. There may be conflicts with oncoming traffic. You may have to slow down, brake and drive to the right or reverse until the road gets wider to allow oncoming traffic drive past you. The roadside of avenues can be particularly uneven because tree roots lift the pavement.
answerASource: I may only drive at a speed that allows me to stop within half of my range of vision
answerBSource: Conflicts can arise with the oncoming traffic
answerCSource: I have to anticipate unevenness at the edge of the carriageway caused by tree roots
questionTextTarget: Dar taşıt yoluna sahip ağaçlıklı bir yolda gidiyorsunuz. Neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Karşı trafik varsa görüş mesafesinin yarısı içinde durabilecek hızda sürmelisiniz. Karşı trafikle çatışmalar olabilir ve ağaç kökleri yol kenarında bozukluk oluşturabilir.
answerATarget: Görüş mesafemin yarısı içinde durabilecek hızla gidebilirim
answerCTarget: Ağaç kökleri yol kenarında düzensizlik oluşturabilir

questionTextSource: What increases the risk of an accident?
explanationSource:
The risk of accidents is significantly increased if your windscreen and side windows are steamed up or iced over, as you can only see the traffic to a limited extent. Clear all windows before you set off.
You could also be dazzled if you drive towards the low sun. This could make you notice other road users later and increase the risk of an accident. Be extra focussed, wear sunglasses or roll down your sun visor.
answerASource: - with the sun low in the sky out front
answerBSource: - with a misted up windscreen
answerCSource: - with iced over side windows
questionTextTarget: Kaza riskini ne artırır?
explanationTarget: Alçakta duran güneş, buğulu ön cam ve buzlu yan camlar görüşü azaltarak kaza riskini artırır.

questionTextSource: You have been driving at high speed for some time. What effects can this have?
explanationSource: You quickly get used to high speeds and you kind of enter a state of a rush. Especially at motorway exits, this can have a fatal impact, as your ability to concentrate and your reaction speed quickly decreases. So, look at the speedometer again and again!
answerASource: You lose your feel for speed
answerBSource: Your powers of concentration diminish
answerCSource: The feel for the right speed on bends grows
questionTextTarget: Bir süredir yüksek hızda gidiyorsunuz. Bunun ne gibi etkileri olabilir?
explanationTarget: Yüksek hıza alışır, hız hissinizi kaybedebilir ve konsantrasyonunuz azalabilir. Virajlar için doğru hız hissi artmaz.

questionTextSource: What can result from driving for a long time at high speed?
explanationSource: The senses have become accustomed to the fast pace and you start to underestimate certain situations. Therefore, drivers often drive at high speed into sharp turns on the freeway exit ramps. The fast and monotonous driving decreases the ability to concentrate and thus the readiness to react. Often the safety distance is neglected and drivers drive too close to the car in front.
answerASource: You lose your feel for speed
answerBSource: You drive too close to vehicles in front
answerCSource: Your reactions improve
questionTextTarget: Uzun süre yüksek hızda sürüş neye yol açabilir?
explanationTarget: Hız hissi kaybolabilir, öndeki araçlara çok yaklaşılabilir ve tepki yeteneği iyileşmez; aksine azalabilir.

questionTextSource: When is there a danger of underestimating your own speed?
explanationSource: The danger of underestimating one's own driving speed comes into place when you made a long journey driving at high speed, for example on the highway. Even wide, clear roads quickly beguile you to drive at high speed. On the other hand, a changing roadside development has the opposite effect.
answerASource: When the road is wide and there are no trees, buildings, fences etc. at the edge of the road
answerBSource: When you have already been driving at high speed for some time
answerCSource: When the roadside features (trees, houses, fences) are constantly changing
questionTextTarget: Kendi hızınızı küçümseme tehlikesi ne zaman vardır?
explanationTarget: Geniş, kenarında ağaç/bina/çit bulunmayan yollarda ve uzun süre yüksek hızla sürdükten sonra hız kolayca küçümsenir. Sürekli değişen yol kenarı bunu azaltır.
answerATarget: Yol genişse ve kenarında ağaç, bina, çit vb. yoksa

questionTextSource: What can lead to your dangerously underestimating your own speed?
explanationSource: Wide and clear roads without changing roadside development can lead to dangerous underestimation of one's own speed. But also, longer trips at higher speeds, such as on the highway, can lead to this effect. On the other hand, strong engine and tire noise let the driver perceive the speed more consciously.
answerASource: Driving for a long time at high speed
answerBSource: Driving on a good, wide road
answerCSource: Loud engine and tyre noise
questionTextTarget: Kendi hızınızı tehlikeli şekilde küçümsemeye ne yol açabilir?
explanationTarget: Uzun süre yüksek hızda sürmek ve iyi, geniş yollarda gitmek hızı küçümsemeye yol açabilir. Yüksek motor ve lastik sesi hızı daha fark edilir kılar.

questionTextSource: You are approaching a narrow left-hand bend at high speed. Why must you reduce speed already before entering the bend?
explanationSource: For you as the car driver there is a risk of skidding at increased speed. In the worst case, you could be thrown out of the bend and lose control of the vehicle. The centrifugal forces are too high at an increased speed. The tires can then no longer transfer the braking forces. That's why you should break early enough.
answerASource: - otherwise you will be thrown out of the bend to the right
answerBSource: - you will go into a skid if you do not brake until you are on the bend
answerCSource: - otherwise you will be thrown out of the bend to the left
questionTextTarget: Yüksek hızla dar bir sol viraja yaklaşıyorsunuz. Viraja girmeden önce neden hızınızı azaltmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Virajda merkezkaç kuvveti sizi sağa dışarı savurabilir. Viraj içinde fren yaparsanız kayabilirsiniz. Sola savrulma beklenmez.
answerATarget: - aksi halde virajdan sağa dışarı savrulursunuz
answerCTarget: - aksi halde virajdan sola dışarı savrulursunuz

questionTextSource: What must you do?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should stay below the speed limit. Children or other pedestrians could run onto the roadway between parked vehicles. In addition, you should expect that the doors of the parked vehicles could suddenly be opened to the side of the road.
answerASource: Drive slowly because children might run out from between the parked vehicles at any time
answerBSource: Drive slowly because a door on one of the vehicles could be opened on the roadway side
answerCSource: Drive at the permitted maximum speed because there is no actual apparent danger
questionTextTarget: Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Park etmiş araçların arasından çocuklar çıkabileceği veya kapılar açılabileceği için yavaş sürmelisiniz. Görünür tehlike yok diye azami hızla gitmek yanlıştır.
answerATarget: Çocuklar her an park etmiş araçların arasından çıkabileceği için yavaş sürmek
answerBTarget: Araçlardan birinin kapısı yol tarafına açılabileceği için yavaş sürmek
answerCTarget: Açık bir tehlike görünmediği için izin verilen azami hızla sürmek

questionTextSource: You are approaching a sharp left-hand bend at high speed. Why is it necessary to reduce your speed before you have actually reached the bend?
explanationSource: Due to your high speed, the centrifugal force may cause your vehicle to skid out of the bend on the right. Therefore, you should reduce your speed before the bend.
answerASource: - could slide off to the right along the bend
answerBSource: - could begin to skid
answerCSource: - could slide off to the left along the bend
questionTextTarget: Yüksek hızla keskin bir sol viraja yaklaşıyorsunuz. Viraja ulaşmadan önce neden hızınızı azaltmanız gerekir?
explanationTarget: Yüksek hızda merkezkaç kuvveti aracı virajın sağ dışına savurabilir ve kayma başlayabilir. Sola savrulma beklenen yön değildir.

questionTextSource: In what situation does a driver frequently underestimate their own speed?
explanationSource: When driving constantly at high speed, the ability to concentrate decreases. You get used to the speed. Also, wide roads with little peripheral development make you less aware of how fast you are actually going. The reaction time is prolonged.
answerASource: On wide, well-finished roads
answerBSource: After a long journey at high speed
answerCSource: On narrow roads with uneven road surfaces
questionTextTarget: Sürücü kendi hızını hangi durumda sık sık küçümser?
explanationTarget: Geniş, iyi yapılmış yollarda ve uzun yüksek hızlı yolculuktan sonra hız kolayca küçümsenir. Dar ve bozuk yollarda genellikle hız daha iyi fark edilir.
answerBTarget: Uzun süre yüksek hızda sürdükten sonra

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when driving at high speed on the motorway?
explanationSource:
Other road users may underestimate your speed and suddenly pull out to overtake, for example. You may not be able to brake in time at high speed if a dangerous situation arises. If you see that danger is imminent, reduce your speed accordingly beforehand. 
In addition, at high speeds, even a slight crosswind can cause your vehicle to veer to the right or left.
answerASource: Other road users underestimating my speed
answerBSource: Cross winds can negatively affect my stability on the road
answerCSource: I might not respond to hazards in good enough time
questionTextTarget: Otoyolda yüksek hızla giderken neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Diğerleri hızınızı küçümseyebilir, yan rüzgâr yol tutuşunuzu bozabilir ve tehlikelere zamanında tepki veremeyebilirsiniz.
answerBTarget: Yan rüzgârın yol tutuşumu olumsuz etkilemesi

questionTextSource: What is especially important when choosing the speed for driving along tree-lined avenues with narrow roadways?
explanationSource: When driving on an avenue with narrow lane width, you must consider the following factors when choosing your speed: Due to the rows of trees on the side, the lighting conditions can change quickly. This makes it difficult for you to estimate the distances to the sides and oncoming traffic. You have to choose your speed so that you can stop halfway on the track, which you can overlook. This is particularly important because wide or tall vehicles often drive in the middle of avenues due to space requirements.
answerASource: To estimate the lateral distance to the trees and oncoming traffic simultaneously
answerBSource: It may be necessary to stop within half the visible distance when a wide vehicle is coming from the opposite direction
answerCSource: Rapidly changing light conditions make it easier to estimate lateral distances
questionTextTarget: Dar taşıt yollu ağaçlıklı yollarda hız seçerken özellikle ne önemlidir?
explanationTarget: Ağaçlar ve karşı trafikle yan mesafeyi aynı anda değerlendirmek zordur. Geniş araç karşıdan gelirse görünür mesafenin yarısı içinde durabilmeniz gerekebilir. Hızlı değişen ışık koşulları mesafe tahminini kolaylaştırmaz.
answerATarget: Ağaçlara ve karşı trafiğe yan mesafeyi aynı anda tahmin etmek
answerBTarget: Karşıdan geniş araç gelirse görünür mesafenin yarısı içinde durmak gerekebilir
answerCTarget: Hızlı değişen ışık koşulları yan mesafeleri tahmin etmeyi kolaylaştırır

questionTextSource: Why are you required to slow down now?
explanationSource: An animal runs on the road from the right. Since there is no vehicle behind you, you should delay, brake and drive to the right. This is the safest way to handle possible scenarios: the tractor could hit the animal and start skidding, the tractor could drive on your lane in an evasive maneuver, you could hit the animal and lose control of your vehicle.
answerASource: Because of the animal
answerBSource: Because of the tractor
answerCSource: Because of the course of the road
questionTextTarget: Şimdi neden yavaşlamanız gerekiyor?
explanationTarget: Sağdan yola çıkan hayvan, traktör ve yolun devamı tehlike oluşturabilir. Hızınızı azaltmanız gerekir.

questionTextSource: Where must you anticipate dangerous situations suddenly arising?
explanationSource: You must be aware of sudden, dangerous situations when: 1. visibility is severely restricted; 2. Persons or animals could unexpectedly appear on the road. In the case of limited visibility, you may detect obstacles such as broken vehicles on the roadway too late. You have less time to react accordingly. In forest areas you have to expect wild animals crossing suddenly during dusk times; in residential areas you have to expect children playing on the street. Caution is also required in front of schools and at school bus stops.
answerASource: In forested areas
answerBSource: After hilltops
answerCSource: In front of schools
questionTextTarget: Ani tehlikeli durumlarla nerelerde karşılaşmayı beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Ormanlık alanlarda, tepe üstlerinden sonra ve okulların önünde insanlar, hayvanlar veya engeller aniden ortaya çıkabilir.

questionTextSource: Why do you have to lower your speed here?
explanationSource: The vehicle overtaking in front of you dangerously carries an accident risk, as it can lead to a collision. In order to prevent participation and also to give the other vehicles space to evade, your own speed must be reduced.
answerASource: Because of the vehicle overtaking
answerBSource: Because of the speed limit
answerCSource: Because of the low volume of traffic
questionTextTarget: Burada neden hızınızı azaltmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Önünüzde sollayan araç tehlikeli bir durum oluşturuyor. Hızı azaltarak kaza riskini düşürür ve kaçınma alanı yaratırsınız.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: With a confusing right turn you have to expect different situations. Especially at dusk times, wild animals can switch sides right after the turn. But it can also happen that oncoming traffic cuts the curve and is recognized very late or that a vehicle is driving at a very low speed right after the curve.
answerASource: Slow-moving vehicle on my side of the road
answerBSource: Fast oncoming traffic cutting the corner
answerCSource: Wild animals crossing the road
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neyi hesaba katmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Görüşü kapalı sağ virajdan sonra sizin tarafınızda yavaş bir araç, virajı kesen hızlı karşı trafik veya yoldan geçen vahşi hayvanlar olabilir.
answerATarget: Benim yol tarafımda yavaş giden araç

questionTextSource: Why do you have to reduce your speed and keep as far to the right as possible here?
explanationSource: In this situation, because of the yellow car coming in the opposite direction, you have to reduce your speed and keep as far to the right as possible
answerASource: Because of the [red car]
answerBSource: Because of the speed limit
answerCSource: Because of the [blue truck]
questionTextTarget: Burada neden hızınızı azaltıp mümkün olduğunca sağda kalmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Karşıdan gelen [sarı] araç nedeniyle hızınızı azaltmalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağda kalmalısınız. Bu durumdaki sebep [kırmızı araba] veya [mavi kamyon] değildir.

questionTextSource: Why can it be dangerous if you, as the last in a queue, immediately overtake as soon as the oncoming traffic is past?
explanationSource: If a convoy has formed, drivers should basically overtake one after the other. The last of a convoy has the longest overtaking path and must also expect other vehicles starting to overtake in front of him.
answerASource: Because you must reckon with others in the queue swinging out to overtake
answerBSource: Because the distance needed to overtake might not be sufficient
answerCSource: Because if you perhaps need to push back into the queue, this could lead to an accident
questionTextTarget: Bir araç kuyruğunda en arkadaysanız ve karşı trafik geçer geçmez hemen sollamaya çıkmanız neden tehlikeli olabilir?
explanationTarget: Öndeki araç kuyruğu oluşmuşsa, sürücüler genelde birbiri ardına sollamalıdır. En arkadaki aracın sollama yolu en uzundur ve ayrıca önündeki başka araçların da sollamaya çıkabileceğini hesaba katmalıdır.
answerATarget: Çünkü kuyruktaki diğer araçların da sollamaya çıkabileceğini hesaba katmalısınız
answerBTarget: Çünkü sollama için gereken mesafe yeterli olmayabilir
answerCTarget: Çünkü gerekirse tekrar kuyruğun içine girmeniz bir kazaya yol açabilir

questionTextSource: What must you remember if you want to overtake?
explanationSource: In an overtaking maneuver you should make sure that the oncoming lane is free. Be sure that there are no bends or gates as well and you only overtake a car. Watch out for more road users, especially trucks.
answerASource: The van could start to overtake the vehicle travelling in front of it
answerBSource: Oncoming traffic could appear
answerCSource: As no oncoming traffic is in sight, you may overtake without hesitation
questionTextTarget: Sollama yapmak istiyorsanız neyi unutmamalısınız?
explanationTarget: Sollama manevrasında karşı şeridin boş olduğundan emin olmalısınız. Ayrıca viraj, kapalı görüş veya başka yol kullanıcıları olup olmadığına dikkat etmelisiniz. Özellikle kamyonlara dikkat edin.
answerCTarget: Karşı trafik görünmediği için tereddütsüz sollayabilirsiniz

questionTextSource: Are you allowed to overtake the black car on this autobahn on the right?
explanationSource: The black car may not be overtaken on the right in this example. On freeways and highways, you may only overtake on the right as an exception. This is the case, for example, when a vehicle queue has formed on the left.
answerASource: No, because there is no queue of vehicles in the left lane
answerBSource: Yes, if you do not drive faster than 80 km/h
answerCSource: Yes, because there is enough space to overtake
questionTextTarget: Bu otoyolda siyah aracı sağdan geçerek sollayabilir misiniz?
explanationTarget: Bu örnekte siyah araç sağdan sollanamaz. Otoyollarda sağdan sollama sadece istisnai durumlarda mümkündür; örneğin sol şeritte araç kuyruğu oluşmuşsa.
answerATarget: Hayır, çünkü sol şeritte araç kuyruğu yok
answerCTarget: Evet, çünkü sollamak için yeterli alan var

questionTextSource: Both cars are travelling at about 30 km/h. Are you allowed to overtake both before the bend?
explanationSource: You may go over the broken line. An overtaking process would still be too dangerous here, because the manageable distance to overtake before the turn is too short. You also have to expect that the driver of the light car will start to overtake.
answerASource: - the distance you need to overtake would be too great
answerBSource: - the light-coloured car could pull out to overtake
answerCSource: - you are not allowed to cross the broken line
questionTextTarget: Her iki araç da yaklaşık 30 km/saat hızla gidiyor. Virajdan önce ikisini de sollayabilir misiniz?
explanationTarget: Kesik çizginin üzerinden geçebilirsiniz. Buna rağmen burada sollama çok tehlikelidir, çünkü virajdan önce güvenli sollama için görülebilen mesafe çok kısadır. Ayrıca açık renkli aracın da sollamaya çıkabileceğini hesaba katmalısınız.
answerATarget: - sollama için gereken mesafe çok uzun olurdu

questionTextSource: The driver of the overtaking car has evidently made a miscalculation. What must you do?
explanationSource: The car driver should reduce the speed. Otherwise an accident could occur. If this is not enough, you could additionally fall back on the grass verge on the right.
answerASource: Brake immediately and veer to the right
answerBSource: Proceed as before
questionTextTarget: Sollayan aracın sürücüsü belli ki yanlış hesap yaptı. Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Araç sürücüsü hızını azaltmalıdır. Aksi halde kaza olabilir. Bu da yetmezse gerekirse sağdaki bankete kaçılabilir.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: As the vehicle behind you has already started the overtaking process, you should refrain from overtaking.
answerASource: I refrain from overtaking
answerBSource: I accelerate
answerCSource: I maintain my speed
questionTextTarget: Sollamak istiyorsunuz. Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Arkanızdaki araç zaten sollamaya başladığı için siz sollamaktan vazgeçmelisiniz.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What should you do?
explanationSource: Here you can overtake if you can see over the required distance and if there is no oncoming vehicle (limited visibility).
answerASource: - only if I have a clear view over the necessary stretch of road
answerBSource: - only if there is no oncoming vehicle
answerCSource: - at the same time, as the [blue car]
questionTextTarget: Sollamak istiyorsunuz. Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Burada ancak gerekli yol kesimini açıkça görebiliyorsanız ve karşıdan araç gelmiyorsa sollayabilirsiniz. Görüş sınırlıdır.
answerATarget: - sadece gerekli yol kesimini net görebiliyorsam

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to overtake here?
explanationSource: In general, you must not overtake if the entire overtaking distance is not visible or the side clearance to other road users cannot be met. Both negative factors are given in the present situation: The assessable track is too short for a safe overtaking maneuver. The minimum distance to the cyclists of 1.5 m cannot be met with oncoming traffic.
answerASource: Because the visible stretch is not sufficient
answerBSource: Because I would not be able to maintain an adequate distance to the side in the event of oncoming traffic
answerCSource: Because I am not permitted to cross the central line
questionTextTarget: Burada neden sollama yapamazsınız?
explanationTarget: Genel olarak, tüm sollama mesafesi görülemiyorsa veya diğer yol kullanıcılarına yandan yeterli mesafe bırakılamıyorsa sollama yapılamaz. Burada iki olumsuz durum da vardır: görülebilen mesafe güvenli sollama için kısa ve karşı trafik varsa bisikletlilere en az 1,5 m yan mesafe bırakılamaz.
answerATarget: Çünkü görülebilen yol kesimi yeterli değil
answerBTarget: Çünkü karşı trafik varsa yandan yeterli mesafeyi koruyamam
answerCTarget: Çünkü orta çizgiyi geçmeme izin yok

questionTextSource: You are being overtaken and the oncoming traffic is getting dangerously close. Despite this the other vehicle continues with the overtaking manoeuvre. What should you do?
explanationSource: You have to slow down and drive to the right, so that the overtaking vehicle driver can go back into lane on time. In no case should you increase the speed if you are overtaken.
answerASource: - reduce my speed
answerBSource: - maintain my speed
answerCSource: - increase my speed
questionTextTarget: Sizi solluyorlar ve karşı trafik tehlikeli şekilde yaklaşıyor. Buna rağmen diğer araç sollamaya devam ediyor. Ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Sollayan sürücünün zamanında kendi şeridine dönebilmesi için yavaşlamalı ve sağa yaklaşmalısınız. Sizi sollarlarken hiçbir durumda hız artırmamalısınız.

questionTextSource: Why should you brake hard now?
explanationSource: Since the blue passenger car will most likely get on your lane, you have to slow down immediately and keep to the right.
answerASource: Because of the car
answerBSource: Because of the truck
answerCSource: Because of the tractor
questionTextTarget: Şimdi neden sert fren yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Mavi otomobil büyük olasılıkla sizin şeridinize gireceği için derhal yavaşlamalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağda kalmalısınız.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: As the car driver, you do not have to dodge to the middle lane when another car pushes you from behind or uses the flash light. You should overtake the blue car and the red van as usual. To change safely to the middle lane, the overtaking process must be completed. Only then you can change.
answerASource: I overtake [the blue car] and then move into the middle lane
answerBSource: I overtake [the red van] and then move into the middle lane
answerCSource: I brake slightly to prompt the vehicle behind to keep its distance
questionTextTarget: Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Arkanızdaki araç selektör yapıyor veya çok yaklaşıyor diye orta şeride kaçmak zorunda değilsiniz. Normal şekilde önce mavi arabayı, sonra kırmızı panelvanı sollamalısınız. Orta şeride güvenle geçebilmek için sollamayı tamamlamış olmanız gerekir.
answerATarget: Önce [mavi arabayı] sollar, sonra orta şeride geçerim
answerBTarget: Önce [kırmızı panelvanı] sollar, sonra orta şeride geçerim
answerCTarget: Arkamdaki aracı mesafeye zorlamak için hafif fren yaparım

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You already indicated and want to overtake the truck. In the rearview mirror you can see a red car approaching on the left lane at high speed. You need to stop the overtaking process immediately and stay behind the truck. If you continue, you force the red car to brake suddenly. He could drive against your car.
answerASource: I cease the overtaking manoeuvre and remain behind the truck
answerBSource: I accelerate and quickly continue the overtaking manoeuvre
answerCSource: I maintain my speed and continue the overtaking manoeuvre
questionTextTarget: Sollamak istiyorsunuz. Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Sollama niyetinizi belirtmiş olsanız bile aynada soldan yüksek hızla yaklaşan kırmızı aracı görüyorsunuz. Sollamayı derhal iptal edip kamyonun arkasında kalmalısınız; devam ederseniz onu ani fren yapmaya zorlarsınız.
answerATarget: Sollamayı bırakır ve kamyonun arkasında kalırım
answerCTarget: Hızımı korur ve sollamaya devam ederim

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: While overtaking a truck, you observe a fast approaching car in the rearview mirror. It is too late to complete the overtaking process. Accelerate and complete the overtaking process quickly but safely. Basically, subsequent vehicles do not have priority; even not on the freeway.
answerASource: I quickly continue the overtaking manoeuvre
answerBSource: I cease the overtaking manoeuvre, and pull back into the right-hand lane
answerCSource: I brake, but continue the overtaking manoeuvre
questionTextTarget: Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Bir kamyonu sollarken aynada hızla yaklaşan bir araç görüyorsunuz. Sollamayı bırakmak için artık çok geçtir. Güvenli ama hızlı şekilde sollamayı tamamlamalısınız. Arkadan gelen araçların, otoyolda bile, otomatik bir önceliği yoktur.
answerBTarget: Sollamayı iptal edip tekrar sağ şeride dönerim
answerCTarget: Fren yaparım ama sollamaya devam ederim

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: A driver drives dangerously close to you on the left-hand lane and signals his/her overtaking intent with his flash light. You should still overtake the transporter on the mid-lane and then get back into the lane in front of it to defuse the situation.
answerASource: I overtake the van and then move into the middle lane
answerBSource: I move immediately into the lane to the right
answerCSource: I brake slightly to prompt the vehicle behind to keep its distance
questionTextTarget: Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Sol şeritte arkanıza çok yaklaşan ve selektör yapan sürücüye rağmen önce orta şeritteki panelvanı sollayıp sonra onun önüne güvenle dönmelisiniz. Bu durum tehlikeyi azaltır.
answerATarget: Panelvanı sollar ve sonra orta şeride geçerim
answerCTarget: Arkamdaki sürücünün mesafe bırakması için hafif fren yaparım

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: As a vehicle is approaching from the front and want to overtake a car behind you (red), you should slow down and extremely drive far right.
answerASource: I slow down
answerBSource: I drive as far to the right-side as possible
answerCSource: I accelerate
questionTextTarget: Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Karşıdan gelen araç, arkanızdaki kırmızı aracı sollamak istiyor. Bu nedenle yavaşlamalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağa gitmelisiniz.

questionTextSource: Why must you slow down here?
explanationSource: The oncoming car overtakes despite opposing traffic about 250 m in front of you. You have to reduce your speed and drive to the far right. So, you defuse the situation and give the overtaking car more time and space to complete the overtaking process. The tractor moves away from the intersection and poses no danger.
answerASource: Because of the overtaking car
answerBSource: Because the maximum permitted speed is 80 km/h
answerCSource: Because of the tractor
questionTextTarget: Burada neden yavaşlamalısınız?
explanationTarget: Karşıdan gelen araç yaklaşık 250 m önünüzde karşı trafik olmasına rağmen sollama yapıyor. Hızınızı azaltmalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağa gitmelisiniz. Böylece durumu yumuşatır ve sollayan araca sollamayı tamamlaması için daha çok zaman ve alan verirsiniz. Traktör tehlike oluşturmuyor.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: If a car overtakes you despite oncoming traffic, you should delay and try to drive to the right as far as possible. This gives the other vehicle enough space to safely complete the overtaking process.
answerASource: I slow down
answerBSource: I drive as far to the right-side as possible
answerCSource: I accelerate
questionTextTarget: Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Bir araç sizi karşı trafik olmasına rağmen solluyorsa yavaşlamalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağa gitmeye çalışmalısınız. Böylece diğer araca sollamayı güvenle tamamlama alanı verirsiniz.

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to overtake in this situation?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to overtake because of the motorbike coming at a fast speed behind you that will overtake you. Also the distance required to overtake is too long in this situation
answerASource: Because the motorbike wants to overtake me
answerBSource: Because my overtaking distance is too long
answerCSource: Because this is a no-overtaking zone
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neden sollama yapamazsınız?
explanationTarget: Arkanızdan hızla gelen ve sizi sollamak isteyen motosiklet nedeniyle ve ayrıca gerekli sollama mesafesi çok uzun olduğu için sollama yapamazsınız.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: Given the truck that is overtaking you has incoming traffic, you should slow down and drive as far to the right as possible to allow him to overtake you
answerASource: I slow down
answerBSource: I drive as far to the right-side as possible
answerCSource: I stop
questionTextTarget: Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Sizi sollayan kamyonun karşıdan gelen trafikle karşılaştığı bu durumda yavaşlamalı ve kamyona alan açmak için mümkün olduğunca sağa gitmelisiniz.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You should not overtake the truck, as you cannot see the distance required for this.
answerASource: I refrain from overtaking the truck, because I do not have a clear view over the necessary stretch of road
answerBSource: I follow the motorbike, because the truck is using its indicators to signal an opportunity to overtake
answerCSource: I reduce my distance to the truck, to enable me to overtake more quickly
questionTextTarget: Sollamak istiyorsunuz. Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Kamyonu sollamamalısınız, çünkü gerekli görüş mesafesi yeterli değil. Motosikleti izlemek veya kamyona yaklaşmak doğru değildir.
answerATarget: Gerekli görüş mesafem olmadığı için kamyonu sollamaktan vazgeçerim
answerBTarget: Kamyon sinyal verdiği için motosikleti takip ederim
answerCTarget: Daha hızlı sollayabilmek için kamyona yaklaşırım

questionTextSource: Why must you abort your overtaking manoeuvre?
explanationSource: You should abort the overtaking maneuver because the tractor wants to turn left and because oncoming traffic may be obstructed.
answerASource: Because the tractor wants to turn left
answerBSource: Because it could obstruct oncoming traffic
answerCSource: Because a no-overtaking zone is visible ahead

questionTextSource: You are driving on a road with one lane for each direction. A convoy of motor vehicles is driving up ahead of you. Why should you refrain from overtaking in order to prevent a dangerous situation from occurring?
explanationSource:
A group of pedestrians, cyclists or motor vehicles, which is traveling together in road traffic, is called a closed association. Closed associations must be clearly marked and must not be interrupted.
Because a closed association consists of several vehicles, you cannot really understand the traffic situation. When overtaking a closed bandage, you should avoid premature inserting, which makes the overtaking path significantly longer.
answerASource: Because the overtaking distance is considerably larger than with single vehicles
answerBSource: Because I should not break the convoy by pulling back into my lane early
answerCSource: Because I cannot assess the road traffic situation ahead of the convoy

questionTextSource: Why is it dangerous to overtake in this situation?

You are driving in a convoy of several cars behind a lorry on a winding road with oncoming traffic. Ideally, the car behind the lorry overtakes first before the vehicles behind move up and start to overtake. 
On the left-hand bend, you cannot see the overtaking lane sufficiently. You will probably have to cancel the overtaking manoeuvre and pull in between two vehicles. There is a possibility that the distance between the vehicles in front is too small and that you will endanger yourself and other road users by pulling in.
answerASource: Because vehicles ahead may swerve out
answerBSource: Because I cannot see far enough along the route necessary for overtaking
answerCSource: Because there is not sufficient space to pull back in between the vehicles ahead

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The motorcyclist is approaching you at high speed. He could overtake you even though the blue truck is already very close. Drive as far to the right as possible to increase the distance to the oncoming lane and reduce your speed so that the motorcyclist can complete the overtaking manoeuvre safely and in good time.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I drive as far to the right-side as possible
answerCSource: I indicate right
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Bu durumda hızınızı azaltmalı ve mümkün olduğunca sağa gitmelisiniz. Sağa sinyal vermek gerekli değildir.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: The yellow car comes towards you as a wrong-way driver on the highway. Drive to the right as far as possible and brake. To warn other road users, turn on the hazard warning lights.
answerASource: I continue driving as far to the right as possible
answerBSource: I brake
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Şimdi ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Mümkün olduğunca sağda kalmaya devam etmeli ve fren yapmalısınız. Sol şeride geçmek doğru değildir.
answerATarget: Mümkün olduğunca sağda gitmeye devam ederim

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Given that you have traffic on the left line behind you, you should slow down and stop behind the taxi and wait for the traffic on the left to clear before overtaking the taxi
answerASource: I stop behind the taxi
answerBSource: I move into the left-hand lane
answerCSource: I sound my horn repeatedly
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Taksinin arkasında durmalı ve gerekirse beklemelisiniz. Sola geçmek veya sürekli korna çalmak doğru değildir.

questionTextSource: What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource:
Animals on the road are unpredictable. You should continue to reduce your speed and monitor the situation carefully, and be ready to brake in case the animals run across the road.
Hazard braking could lead to a rear-end collision if the vehicle behind you cannot react quickly enough.
answerASource: - reduce my speed further
answerBSource: - be ready to brake
answerCSource: - perform an emergency braking manoeuvre
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Hızınızı daha da azaltmalı ve fren yapmaya hazır olmalısınız. Hemen tam fren yapmak zorunlu değildir.

questionTextSource: Why should you reduce your speed now?
explanationSource: Even if you keep to the speed limit of 60 km/h, an animal will run onto the road right in front of you. To avoid an accident, you have to reduce your speed. The oncoming car does not affect your driving style.
answerASource: Because of the animal
answerBSource: Because of the [red car]
answerCSource: Because of the speed limit
questionTextTarget: Şimdi neden hızınızı azaltmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Hayvan nedeniyle hızınızı azaltmalısınız. Kırmızı araç veya hız sınırı bunun nedeni değildir.

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource:
The driver of the yellow car wants to turn left. He may overlook you behind the tractor. Expect this and be ready to brake.
You should also take care not to drive on the chippings, as chippings increase the risk of skidding on the carriageway.
answerASource: The driver of the [yellow car] may fail to see me
answerBSource: The loose chippings on the carriageway mean there is an increased risk of crashing
answerCSource: I have to allow right of way to the [red car]
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Sarı aracın sürücüsü sizi görmeyebilir ve yol üzerindeki gevşek mıcır kaza riskini artırır. Kırmızı araca öncelik vermeniz gerekmez.
answerBTarget: Taşıt yolundaki gevşek mıcır nedeniyle kaza riski artar

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: As the blue car will probably be travelling on the road without observing the right of way rules, you must slow down sharply and give up your right of way.
answerASource: I brake sharply
answerBSource: I yield my right of way
answerCSource: I continue driving as before
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Önceliğiniz olsa bile beyaz araç durmazsa hızınızı azaltmalı ve önceliğinizden vazgeçmelisiniz.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The tractor is signalling by flashing its lights that it wants to move back into the lane. You should therefore reduce your speed and allow the tractor to merge into the lane.
answerASource: I reduce my speed further
answerBSource: I allow the tractor to pull into the lane
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Hızınızı daha da azaltmalı ve traktörün şeride girmesine izin vermelisiniz. Sol şeride geçmek gerekli değildir.

questionTextSource: What is the right thing to do in this situation?
explanationSource: On the narrow country road, it is possible that you and the tractor will not be able to pass each other easily. Be prepared to stop and let the tractor pass.
answerASource: I must stop if necessary
answerBSource: I have to steer to the right onto the field as a precaution
answerCSource: I have priority over the tractor
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda doğru olan nedir?
explanationTarget: Gerekirse durmalısınız. Önlem olarak tarlaya kaçmak zorunda değilsiniz. Traktöre karşı mutlaka önceliğiniz olduğu varsayımıyla davranmamalısınız.
answerBTarget: Önlem olarak sağa tarlaya kırmam gerekir

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource:
The yellow car has an unusual driving style. It may be distracted or drunk.
The abrupt braking and steering manoeuvres of the car in front are dangerous. Pay attention to how he continues to drive, increase the distance and be ready to brake.
answerASource: I increase my distance to the [car] ahead
answerBSource: I continue driving with increased care
answerCSource: I overtake [the car ahead] leaving a large side clearance
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Öndeki araçla aranızdaki mesafeyi artırmalı ve artan dikkatle devam etmelisiniz. Sollamak doğru değildir.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when driving along narrow avenues?
explanationSource:
Visibility in avenues is more restricted than in open fields and therefore requires an adapted driving style.
On bends, your view of the carriageway may be restricted after the bend. You may therefore only see obstacles very late.
answerASource: - it is frequently difficult for me to see into the road bends
answerBSource: - I have to drive particularly defensively
answerCSource: - I am protected against black ice
questionTextTarget: Dar ağaçlıklı yollarda sürerken neye dikkat etmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Virajların içi sık sık iyi görünmez ve bu nedenle özellikle savunmacı sürmelisiniz. Bu yollar siyah buza karşı koruma sağlamaz.
answerATarget: - virajların içini görmek çoğu zaman zordur

questionTextSource: What is the right behaviour now?
explanationSource:
There is no other option for you in this situation other than to pull over behind the taxi. Because you are not allowed to pass to the right of the taxi as this is a sidewalk. Driving on a sidewalk is generally prohibited.
You also can't change to the left lane because there is a motorcycle next to you. If you change lane you risk a collision.
answerASource: I stop behind the taxi
answerBSource: I moving into the left-hand lane
answerCSource: I carefully drive past the taxi on the right
questionTextTarget: Şimdi doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Taksinin arkasında durmalısınız. Sola geçmek veya sağdan dikkatlice geçmek doğru değildir.

questionTextSource: You are taking part in a group tour with several motorbikes. What is the right behaviour?
explanationSource:
Your position in the group should be decided before the group ride and should not change during the ride to avoid constant position changes. You should always pay attention to the necessary safety distance from the motorcycle in front of you.
This prevents potentially dangerous situations with other motorcycles or oncoming traffic and allows you to react in a timely manner to dangers or sudden changes in speed.
answerASource: I keep my position in the group
answerBSource: I make sure I keep a safe distance from the motorbike in front
answerCSource: I always ride as fast as the motorbike in front
questionTextTarget: Birkaç motosikletle yapılan bir grup turuna katılıyorsunuz. Doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Gruptaki yerinizi korumalı ve öndeki motosiklete güvenli mesafe bırakmalısınız. Her zaman öndeki kadar hızlı gitmek gerekmez.
answerCTarget: Her zaman öndeki motosiklet kadar hızlı sürerim

questionTextSource: What could cause a fast travelling motor vehicle suddenly to veer sideways on a dry straight road?
explanationSource: A vehicle on a straight, dry road can swing off sideways in the event of a strong crosswind or a burst tire. In this case, immediately lay off the gas pedal, countersteer and brake gently.
answerASource: It is suddenly hit by a gust of strong side wind
answerBSource: A burst tyre
answerCSource: Sudden headwind
questionTextTarget: Kuru ve düz bir yolda hızlı giden bir motorlu aracın aniden yana savrulmasına ne sebep olabilir?
explanationTarget: Güçlü yan rüzgâr darbeleri ve lastik patlaması aracın yana savrulmasına neden olabilir. Sürekli karşı rüzgâr bu etkiyi yapmaz.

questionTextSource: What makes driving in a stream of vehicles easier?
explanationSource: Slightly offset driving allows you to observe the vehicles in front. This allows you to see the traffic situation in front of you or through the windows of the driver in front and react faster to sudden events. Driving with hazard lights in a convoy is generally prohibited.
answerASource: Watching the queue through the windows of the vehicles travelling in front
answerBSource: Driving in a slightly staggered position in your own lane
answerCSource: Keeping your hazard warning lights switched on when driving
questionTextTarget: Araç akışı içinde sürüşü ne kolaylaştırır?
explanationTarget: Öndeki araçların camlarından ileriye bakmak ve kendi şeridinizde hafif şaşırtmalı konum almak yardımcı olur. Sürerken dörtlüleri yakmak doğru değildir.
answerBTarget: Kendi şeridimde hafif şaşırtmalı konumda gitmek
answerCTarget: Sürüş sırasında dörtlü ikazları açık tutmak

questionTextSource: What must you do after an accident with a game animal (for example a deer)?
explanationSource: After a wildlife accident, you must stop, turn on your hazard lights and secure the scene of the accident. You also have to inform the police or the nearest forestry office about the accident. Under no circumstances should you touch the game with your bare hands due to the danger of rabies!
answerASource: Stop, switch on hazard warning lights and make the scene of the accident safe
answerBSource: Inform the police or the nearest forestry office
answerCSource: Proceed without stopping
questionTextTarget: Yaban hayvanıyla (örneğin geyik) çarpışmadan sonra ne yapmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Durmalı, dörtlüleri yakmalı ve kaza yerini emniyete almalısınız. Ayrıca polisi veya en yakın orman idaresini bilgilendirmelisiniz.
answerATarget: Durur, dörtlüleri yakar ve kaza yerini emniyete alırım
answerBTarget: Polisi veya en yakın orman idaresini bilgilendiririm

questionTextSource: One of your tyres bursts while you are travelling at high speed on an autobahn. What do you do?
explanationSource: Avoid emergency braking on the freeway and instead steer and brake gently to stabilize your vehicle. If possible, bring your vehicle to a stop on the hard shoulder. Turn on your hazard lights and set up your warning triangle to warn subsequent vehicles.
answerASource: Throttle down, if necessary, steer against and brake cautiously
answerBSource: Stop, if possible, on the hard shoulder, switch on hazard warning lights and place the warning triangle
answerCSource: Immediately apply the brakes fully and move over onto the hard shoulder
questionTextTarget: Otoyolda yüksek hızla giderken lastiklerinizden biri patladı. Ne yaparsınız?
explanationTarget: Gazı bırakmalı, gerekirse karşı direksiyon yapmalı ve dikkatlice frenlemelisiniz. Mümkünse emniyet şeridinde durmalı, dörtlüleri yakmalı ve uyarı üçgeni koymalısınız. Tam fren yaparak emniyet şeridine atlamak yanlıştır.
answerATarget: Gazı keser, gerekirse karşı direksiyon yapar ve dikkatlice frenlerim
answerBTarget: Mümkünse emniyet şeridinde durur, dörtlüleri yakar ve uyarı üçgenini koyarım
answerCTarget: Hemen tam fren yapar ve emniyet şeridine geçerim

questionTextSource: You increase your speed from 50 km/h to 100 km/h. How is the braking distance changed according to the rule of thumb?
explanationSource: According to the rule of thumb, the braking distance quadruples when the speed doubles. At 40 km/h, for example, it is 16 meters and at 80 km/h 64 meters.
answerASource: It is quadrupled from 25 m to 100 m
answerBSource: It is doubled from 15 m to 30 m
answerCSource: It is halved from 50 m to 25 m
questionTextTarget: Hızınızı 50 km/saatten 100 km/saat’e çıkarıyorsunuz. Kural olarak fren mesafesi nasıl değişir?
explanationTarget: Kural olarak hız iki katına çıktığında fren mesafesi dört katına çıkar; 25 m’den 100 m’ye.

questionTextSource: When leaving a built-up area, both you and the vehicle approximately 15 m ahead of you increase speed from 50 km/h to 100 km/h. How should you change the safety distance between you and the vehicle ahead?
explanationSource: Keep a safe distance from the vehicle in front of you outside of closed areas that is at least half the speed in meters. At 100 km/h, the safety distance should therefore be at least 50 meters.
answerASource: - increase to at least 50 m
answerBSource: - remain the same
answerCSource: - increase to at least 25 m
questionTextTarget: Yerleşim yerinden çıkarken siz ve yaklaşık 15 m önünüzdeki araç hızınızı 50 km/saatten 100 km/saat’e çıkarıyorsunuz. Güvenli takip mesafesini nasıl değiştirmelisiniz?
explanationTarget: 100 km/saatte güvenli takip mesafesi en az 50 m olmalıdır.

questionTextSource: Why must you be ready to brake in this situation?
explanationSource: You see that a vehicle wants to drive onto the road from the right. Since it comes from a field or forest path, you have right of way. Due to the forest cover the visibility for the vehicle driver may be limited. It is possible that he/she will continue despite having to wait. For this reason, you must be ready to brake until you clearly recognize that he/she is stopping.
answerASource: Because the other vehicle might continue moving
answerBSource: Because the direction of the road ahead demands it
answerCSource: Because a speed limit is visible
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neden fren yapmaya hazır olmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Öndeki diğer araç hareketine devam edebilir. Ayrıca yolun gidişi de dikkat gerektirir; görülen hız sınırı tek başına neden değildir.
answerATarget: Çünkü diğer araç hareketine devam edebilir

questionTextSource: Why should you lower your speed now?
explanationSource: Basically, you have the right of way, because you are on a priority road. However, to avoid a dangerous situation, you should still reduce your speed and be ready to brake, as the car driver of the car coming from the right-hand side might recognize you too late or miss you altogether. Due to the trees on the roadside his/her view is clearly limited.
answerASource: Because I am badly visible to other road users
answerBSource: Because the direction of the road ahead demands it
answerCSource: Because I have to give right of way
questionTextTarget: Şimdi neden hızınızı azaltmalısınız?
explanationTarget: Diğer yol kullanıcıları tarafından kötü görüldüğünüz için ve yolun gidişi bunu gerektirdiği için yavaşlamalısınız. Bu durumda öncelik vermek zorunda değilsiniz.
answerATarget: Çünkü diğer yol kullanıcıları beni kötü görüyor

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: In this situation, you need to reduce the speed and allow the car driver to go back into lane on the left. If a lane ends on a multi-lane road or if it is not possible for the drivers driving on the other lane to drive through the lane continuously, they must be able to change lanes using the zipper procedure.
answerASource: I slow down
answerBSource: I accelerate
answerCSource: I continue driving as before
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda doğru davranış nedir?
explanationTarget: Yavaşlamalısınız. Hızlanmak ya da olduğu gibi devam etmek doğru değildir.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: Although you are on the priority road, you must always expect dangerous situations to arise. For example, the tractor driver approaching your street at a junction could not fulfill his duty of waiting and force the vehicle in front of you to brake. By driving anticipatory you can help defuse potentially dangerous situations.
answerASource: - motorcyclist will slow down
answerBSource: - tractor will proceed onto the road
answerCSource: - tractor will wait
questionTextTarget: Bu durumda neyi beklemelisiniz?
explanationTarget: Motosikletli yavaşlayabilir, traktör ise yola çıkabilir. Traktörün kesinlikle bekleyeceğini varsayamazsınız.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: The maximum speed limit on this road is 80 km/h. You drive 80 km/h.
